channel.title: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism.
channel.description: KillBait is a news aggregator developed by students and professionals in journalism and web development from Argentina, Spain, and the United Kingdom. Our focus is on providing readers with clear, concise, and relevant summaries of each news story.

item.title: What is KillBait?
item.description: Surely you've clicked on a news article with an eye-catching or sensational headline...

item.title: How KillBait works
item.description: Our editors, along with any registered user, can submit news they find interesting simply by providing the URL to the system.

item.title: Frecuently Asked Questions
item.description: Common Questions about KillBait

item.title: Top rated media
item.description: Best media: This classification is based on the votes cast by the users of this website on each news item from different media, and it does not necessarily reflect reality.

item.description: Worst media: This classification is based on the votes cast by the users of this website on each news item from different media, and it does not necessarily reflect reality.

item.title: Upcoming news
Iran Reports Execution of Revolutionary Guard General Amid Allegations of Espionage
item.description: Iran has reportedly executed General Ismail Qaani, commander of the Quds Force within the Revolutionary Guard, over alleged long-term collaboration with Israel's Mossad. Qaani, aged 67, is said to have provided intelligence on the whereabouts of Iran's supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, before an Israeli attack. Sources claim he survived multiple targeted attacks against Iranian and allied leaders, raising suspicions of espionage. Reports suggest Chinese military intelligence intercepted communications linking Qaani to U.S. and Israeli intelligence. Over the years, numerous high-ranking figures in the so-called Resistance Axis, including Hezbollah and Hamas leaders, were killed in operations that Qaani allegedly narrowly escaped. Qaani assumed leadership of the Quds Force in 2020 following the U.S. killing of Qassem Suleimani. While some reports have not independently verified his execution, the general’s repeated survival in life-threatening incidents has fueled speculation of insider betr

item.title: Hayden AI Sues Former CEO Over Alleged Data Theft and Resume Fraud
item.description: Hayden AI, a San Francisco-based startup specializing in spatial analytics for urban planning, has filed a lawsuit against its former co-founder and CEO, Chris Carson. The company alleges that in the days leading up to his removal in September 2024, Carson engaged in a series of fraudulent activities. The lawsuit claims he secretly copied 41GB of company emails, forged board signatures, sold more than $1.2 million in company stock without authorization, and misallocated personal expenses. Additionally, Hayden AI accuses Carson of falsifying key parts of his resume, including claims of a PhD from Waseda University and military service, which the company asserts are entirely fabricated. The startup, currently valued at an estimated $464 million, is seeking preliminary injunctive relief to ensure that the allegedly stolen data is returned or destroyed. Commenters have noted that the situation reflects broader issues in the AI industry, including insufficient due diligence in hiring and th

item.title: Top Adviser to NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani Advises British Progressive Politicians on Campaign Strategies
item.description: Morris Katz, a 26-year-old key strategist behind New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's successful 2025 mayoral campaign, traveled to the United Kingdom in February 2026 to meet with progressive politicians from the Labour and Green parties. Katz, who has not joined Mamdani's administration but remains a close adviser, met with figures including Labour MPs Rosie Wrighting and Gordon McKee, as well as Rowenna Davis, a Labour candidate for mayor of Croydon. The meetings, initiated by UK operatives, focused on campaign strategies, messaging, and social media tactics that helped Mamdani win by emphasizing affordability issues like free public transit and childcare, funded by taxes on the wealthy. Katz described his efforts as part of an international fight against oligarchic and far-right interests. He has continued virtual consultations and plans further engagement with Green Party leadership, though his UK work was unpaid and he is prioritizing U.S. midterm elections. This reflects growing

item.title: National Democrats Hesitate on Major Spending for Texas Senate Race
item.description: National Democrats are grappling with whether to invest heavily in Texas to support James Talarico's U.S. Senate campaign amid recent primary successes. Talarico, a state representative who won the Democratic nomination, has sparked optimism after strong primary results, but party strategists question the high cost of competing in the historically Republican state. A Democratic consultant noted that $150 million for Texas might be better allocated to cheaper battlegrounds like Iowa, Montana, or Nebraska to secure House and Senate majorities. Senate Majority PAC emphasized that while paths to victory run through states like Ohio and North Carolina, Texas remains in play. Talarico's campaign has raised significant funds from grassroots donors, positioning him as a populist fighter against billionaire influence, though Republicans mock his chances and highlight his progressive stances on issues like borders and gender. Some GOP insiders privately acknowledge the risk, citing recent spendi

item.title: Research Highlights Underdiagnosis of Sleep Apnea in Women and the Need for Targeted Detection
item.description: Sleep apnea, once thought to primarily affect older, heavier men, is increasingly recognized as a significant and often undetected condition in women, especially during perimenopause and menopause. Obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) occurs when the upper airway collapses during sleep, causing repeated oxygen drops and brief awakenings. Hormonal changes, particularly the decline in estrogen and progesterone after menopause, increase women’s susceptibility to OSA by reducing airway protection and altering fat distribution. Women may experience symptoms differently than men, often presenting with fatigue, insomnia, mood changes, headaches, or restless sleep, rather than classic loud snoring or excessive daytime sleepiness. These subtle presentations frequently lead to misdiagnosis or delayed diagnosis, as existing screening tools were largely developed based on male or mixed populations. Even mild apnea, with roughly 15 breathing disruptions per hour, can have serious health consequences, incl

item.title: Selena Gomez playfully kisses Benny Blanco’s foot during podcast appearance
item.description: During a recent episode of Benny Blanco’s podcast 'Friends Keep Secrets,' Selena Gomez surprised listeners with a playful gesture by kissing her husband’s foot in the middle of a conversation. The 33-year-old singer was seated on the floor next to Blanco, who was relaxing on the couch, when she leaned over and kissed his index toe. Blanco paused briefly before smiling and joking with co-hosts Lil Dicky (Dave Burd) and Kristin Batalucco. The moment was lighthearted, with Gomez playfully slapping Blanco’s leg and continuing the affectionate exchange by resting her cheek against his toes. The podcast episode also touched on the couple’s relationship, with Gomez praising their bond as 'healthy' and 'one of the greatest things in life,' while Blanco explained he usually keeps public displays of affection minimal to allow Gomez to maintain professionalism during interviews. The playful foot-kissing moment followed earlier online attention on Blanco’s feet after viewers noticed them looking d

item.title: Christina Applegate recalls leaving Brad Pitt for Sebastian Bach at the 1989 MTV Awards
item.description: In her memoir 'You With the Sad Eyes,' Christina Applegate recounts a pivotal moment from the 1989 MTV Video Music Awards. At 17, during the height of her fame from 'Married… with Children,' she attended the Los Angeles ceremony with Brad Pitt as her date. Applegate describes feeling confident and glamorous in a Ceil Chapman gown, which gave her the courage to leave Pitt at the event and walk out with Skid Row frontman Sebastian Bach instead. She reflects on the dynamic at the time, noting that Pitt had not yet achieved major stardom and was still establishing his acting career, which would later explode with 'Thelma & Louise' in 1991. According to Applegate, Pitt was understandably upset and had to drive her mother and a friend home, leading to years of minimal contact between them. Eventually, the two reconciled, with Pitt forgiving her youthful decision. Applegate had previously hinted at this story during a 2015 interview on 'Watch What Happens Live,' without revealing Bach’s ident

item.title: U.S. Military Presence in Gulf States Increases Regional Security Risks
item.description: Before recent U.S.-Iran tensions escalated, Gulf countries believed hosting American military bases would strengthen their security by deterring attacks. However, recent events have shown that these bases primarily serve U.S. strategic interests rather than the protection of host nations. The presence of American infrastructure has inadvertently exposed Gulf states to potential Iranian retaliation, undermining their security. While no Gulf countries have yet retaliated against Iran, some have considered it, with Saudi Arabia positioned as the regional leader capable of coordinating potential military action with the United States. Regional rivalries, such as that between Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, could influence whether coordinated action occurs. Gulf states share a common concern about perceived Iranian aggression, which is also framed in historical and sectarian terms following the 1979 Iranian Revolution and subsequent geopolitical developments like the Abraham Acco

item.title: Asian Stock Markets Plunge Amid Rising Middle East Tensions
item.description: Asian stock markets have experienced significant drops as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East escalate. The conflict involving Iran has triggered sharp declines across major Asian indices, with Seoul's Kospi index falling nearly 10%, marking its largest drop in 15 years since the conflict began. Measures to temporarily halt trading could not prevent losses. In Tokyo, the Nikkei fell 4%, while Hong Kong and Singapore saw decreases of 3.1% and 2.8% respectively. Key corporations, heavily reliant on Gulf oil and natural gas, also suffered; Hyundai dropped almost 14%, LG Energy Solutions fell 10%, and Samsung declined 9.2%. Japanese firms including Toyota, SoftBank, and Advantest also faced notable losses. The instability reflects the economic vulnerabilities of countries dependent on Middle Eastern oil, particularly as the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical shipping route. Investors reacted to Iran's unexpected military responses and the broadening of regional operations to neighbor

item.title: Trump Supporters in Pennsylvania Show Mixed Reactions to Military Action Against Iran
item.description: Residents in Gettysburg, Pennsylvania, a Republican stronghold, have shown divided opinions following U.S. military strikes against Iran under President Donald Trump. Some voters express concern that the actions contradict Trump's campaign promises to avoid foreign conflicts, emphasizing that their support was based on an 'America First' anti-war stance. Critics argue that the strikes could put American lives at risk and clash with the administration’s principle of 'peace through strength.' Conversely, other supporters view the military action as necessary, framing it as a protective measure that aligns with confronting dangerous regimes and prioritizing U.S. interests. The issue also resonates beyond traditional Republican voters, including members of the Iranian diaspora and centrists who support strong action against Iran's leadership. National polling indicates broader skepticism about the strikes, with 43% of Americans opposing the moves, suggesting potential political challenges

item.title: Billy Idol Discusses Overcoming Heroin Addiction and Life Lessons
item.description: Rock icon Billy Idol recently opened up about his struggles with drug addiction, explaining that during his battle with heroin, he briefly turned to crack as a way to stop using heroin. Idol shared these revelations during a podcast appearance on Club Random with Bill Maher, emphasizing that while it was a dangerous choice, it ultimately helped him break free from heroin. In his new documentary, 'Billy Idol Should Be Dead,' the singer reflects on his tumultuous career, near-death experiences, and reckless lifestyle, including a severe motorcycle accident in 1990 and a near-fatal heroin overdose in 1984. Idol recounted how drug use was deeply ingrained in the 1980s rock scene, beginning with his first experience with acid at just 12 years old. Over time, fatherhood and self-reflection led him to adopt a more disciplined lifestyle. Today, he describes himself as 'California sober,' occasionally using cannabis-based products but avoiding cocaine for more than 20 years. The documentary pre

item.title: Military Forces Increasingly Exploit Civilian Security Cameras for Reconnaissance
item.description: Recent research by the security firm Check Point reveals that civilian security cameras are increasingly being targeted as tools for military reconnaissance in conflicts from Ukraine to Iran. Iranian state-linked hackers reportedly attempted to hijack hundreds of consumer-grade cameras, coinciding with missile and drone strikes in Israel, Qatar, and Cyprus. Similar tactics have been used by Israel, Russia, and Ukraine, demonstrating a growing reliance on inexpensive, networked civilian devices to gather real-time intelligence. The attacks exploited known vulnerabilities in Hikvision and Dahua cameras, many of which were years old but remained unpatched due to users neglecting updates. These hacked cameras provide militaries with detailed ground-level surveillance that can be used to plan strikes, track enemy movements, and assess damage after attacks. Experts note that this method is cheaper, stealthier, and sometimes more informative than traditional reconnaissance tools such as satel

item.title: Tate McRae and Jack Hughes Are in an Exclusive Relationship
item.description: Pop singer Tate McRae, 22, and Team USA hockey player Jack Hughes, 24, have reportedly started dating exclusively, according to a source cited by Us Weekly. The couple was first spotted together in New York City in December, and they have been spending time together since late last year. The source confirmed that the relationship is still in its early stages but is progressing positively, with McRae describing it as going well so far. Hughes reportedly initiated contact via Instagram, and McRae finds him both 'really cool' and 'really sweet.' They have enjoyed shared activities, and McRae has been attending Hughes' hockey games to support him. Hughes recently gained international attention for scoring the gold medal-winning goal for Team USA in men’s ice hockey at the Winter Olympics, marking the country’s first gold in the sport since 1980. Following the match, images of Hughes celebrating with a bloodied smile and a broken front tooth circulated widely, though he has joked that the i

item.title: Cardi B and Megan Thee Stallion Reunite for Surprise Performance in Houston
item.description: During her Little Miss Drama Tour stop in Houston on March 4, 2026, Cardi B delighted fans by bringing Megan Thee Stallion on stage for a surprise performance. The concert, held at the Toyota Center, featured songs from both Cardi B’s debut album 'Invasion of Privacy' and her latest record 'Am I the Drama,' which inspired the tour’s title. The highlight of the night came when Megan Thee Stallion joined Cardi for their chart-topping hit 'WAP,' sending the crowd into an uproar. The collaboration had particular resonance in Houston, as it is Megan’s hometown, making the moment a personal triumph for both artists and their fans. In addition to 'WAP,' the pair also delivered a live performance of their recent single 'Bongos,' further energizing the atmosphere. The appearance added to the growing list of joint performances between the two rap stars, following a similar surprise in 2024 when Cardi appeared on stage with Megan during her Hot Girl Summer Tour in New York. Their repeated collabo

item.title: Heatherwick Studio Proposes Curved Residential Towers for Seoul’s Daegyo Apartments
item.description: Heatherwick Studio has unveiled an innovative plan to redevelop the Daegyo Apartments in Yeouido, Seoul. The project aims to replace the aging 1975-era complex with four new residential towers featuring soft, rolling rooflines inspired by the nearby mountains. Departing from the conventional straight high-rise design, the proposed towers emphasize curves and texture to create a warmer, more inviting residential environment. The redevelopment would expand the site from 576 homes to approximately 900, with significant input from hundreds of resident union members. At street level, the design focuses on calm, pedestrian-friendly spaces, incorporating raised site edges to buffer traffic and sunken courtyards to offer tranquil urban retreats. The ground floor is intended to be accessible to both residents and the public, integrating community facilities such as play areas and welfare services. By blending architectural innovation with community-oriented planning, the project aims to provide

item.title: Analysis of U.S. Patriot Missile Inventory and Potential Shortages
item.description: The United States currently relies on the PAC-3 MSE (Patriot Advanced Capability-3 Missile with Enhanced Segment) as its primary interceptor for high-priority threats. Production of this missile began in small quantities in 2014, scaling up to full production in 2018. From 2015 to 2025, a total of approximately 4,620 PAC-3 MSE missiles were produced, with 847 transferred to Ukraine. Assuming none were used elsewhere, this leaves around 3,773 in U.S. inventory. Military analysts estimate that in a high-intensity conflict scenario like the hypothetical '12-Day War,' with Iran launching 60 ballistic missiles per day and two interceptors fired per incoming missile, the U.S. would exhaust its stock in roughly 10 days. These calculations assume all missiles are deployed in Israel and nearby Gulf states, though in reality, some are stationed in other theaters such as INDOPACOM and EUCOM. The analysis highlights potential vulnerabilities in U.S. missile defense capabilities if faced with susta

item.title: The Decline of Western Influence in the Middle East and the Risks of Escalation
item.description: The article analyzes the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, emphasizing Iran's resilience against Western and Israeli interventions. The author argues that Iran will not collapse like Iraq, Syria, or Libya, due to its long-standing civilization and societal strength, even if its current regime falls. The piece critiques the U.S. strategy, particularly the assassination of Iran's leader, labeling it a tactical but strategically disastrous move. The author highlights the fragility of Western-backed local regimes and the limitations of imperial protection, noting that missile and drone attacks demonstrate the vulnerability of allied nations. Israel is portrayed as pursuing an aggressive regional strategy that is unsustainable in the long term due to its small population, lack of resources, and dependence on Western support. The article predicts that failures in Iran could accelerate the collapse of U.S. influence and the petrodollar system, while the potential use of nuclear weapons wou

item.title: Roman Water Systems and Public Hygiene: How Infrastructure Sustained the Empire
item.description: Long before modern plumbing, ancient Rome had developed an advanced water and sanitation system that set it apart from most of the world. Starting in 312 B.C. with the construction of the Aqua Appia, Romans brought clean water from distant springs into the city through underground aqueducts, eventually building a network of eleven major aqueducts delivering over a million cubic meters of water daily. This water supplied public fountains, private homes, baths, and gardens, creating a continuous flow system rather than mere storage. The distribution was carefully managed, with the curator aquarum overseeing allocation and addressing issues such as illegal diversions and theft by wealthy citizens. Public baths, numbering over nine hundred by the fourth century, were centers of hygiene, socializing, and leisure, highlighting the cultural importance of water. Equally vital was the Cloaca Maxima, a massive sewer system that drained rainwater, domestic waste, and bath water, making the city l

item.title: Emma Watson Seen Socializing with Entrepreneur Gonzalo Hevia Baillères
item.description: Emma Watson has been spotted multiple times with Mexican entrepreneur Gonzalo Hevia Baillères, sparking public interest in a potential romantic connection. Sightings of the pair began circulating in early 2026 after attending a wedding in Mexico together. More recently, they were photographed at an airport in March by the Mexican magazine Quién, as well as in locations such as Courchevel in the French Alps, Punta Mita on Mexico’s Pacific coast, and various sites in Mexico City. One image circulating online appears to show them displaying affection publicly, though neither Watson nor Hevia Baillères has confirmed any relationship. Gonzalo Hevia Baillères comes from one of Mexico’s wealthiest families, including ties to mining company Industrias Peñoles, Grupo BAL, and the luxury department store chain El Palacio de Hierro. Forbes ranks his family among the richest in Latin America, with estimated wealth between $6.65 billion and $19.7 billion. Hevia Baillères has pursued his own venture

item.title: US Justice Department Releases Previously Missing FBI Interview Summaries Containing Unverified Allegations Against Trump from Epstein Files
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has released additional FBI documents that were absent from the initial public batch of Jeffrey Epstein files, following reports by media outlets including NBC News. These files, which had been incorrectly marked as 'duplicative' in the department's spreadsheet, include summaries and notes from three 2019 FBI interviews (conducted on August 7, August 20, and October 16) with a woman from South Carolina. She alleged that Jeffrey Epstein sexually assaulted her on Hilton Head Island around 1984 when she was 13, and claimed further abuse by Epstein and several associates, including then-president Donald Trump. Only the summary of the initial July 24, 2019 interview was in the earlier release and made no mention of Trump. The woman’s name is redacted throughout the documents. The Justice Department reiterated warnings that the Epstein files contain unverified, false, or sensationalist claims—some submitted just before the 2020 election—and emphasized that thes

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Blockade Threatens Global Aluminum Supply
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz is severely impacting the global aluminum market. The blockade threatens both the supply of aluminum to the world and the delivery of alumina to smelters in the Persian Gulf. Stock prices are falling while aluminum prices are rising, with the price per ton increasing from $3,140 to $3,300, a 5% jump, nearing levels last seen in 2022. Gulf countries are key producers, accounting for 8.3% of global primary aluminum production and 14.5% of global exports. Energy costs, particularly natural gas, directly affect aluminum production costs, and the region’s largest producer, the United Arab Emirates, is heavily dependent on the Strait of Hormuz for exports. Even minor disruptions can create supply chain challenges for industries from automotive manufacturing to beverage cans. European, U.S., and Asian markets are already paying higher prices for alternative supply. Qatar’s public energy company announced a halt in aluminum production due to domestic gas sho

item.title: Trump Announces Potential US Naval Escorts and Financial Guarantees to Resume Shipping Through the Strait of Hormuz Amid Iranian Threats
item.description: On Tuesday, March 3, 2026, President Donald Trump announced plans to form naval escorts 'as soon as possible' to help stranded vessels in the Persian Gulf transit the Strait of Hormuz, which has seen severely reduced traffic due to heightened risks from the ongoing conflict involving Iran. He also pledged to provide financial guarantees at reasonable prices through the US International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) to cover risks that private insurers are unwilling to take, aiming to restore commercial shipping flows. However, less than 24 hours earlier, US Navy officials stated there was no realistic possibility of deploying escort ships and questioned their effectiveness against potential Iranian threats. The article draws parallels to the failed 2023 US and EU operations in the Red Sea against Houthi forces during the Gaza conflict, suggesting similar efforts would likely fail against Iran. Normally, 100 to 120 vessels (including tankers, LNG carriers, chemical ships, contai

item.title: Iran's Mosaic Defense Strategy: Designed to Prolong Conflict and Counter Rapid Decapitation Strikes by the US and Israel
item.description: The article examines Iran's 'mosaic defense' (defensa en mosaico descentralizada), a decentralized military architecture developed since the early 2000s to thwart quick, decisive victories by technologically superior adversaries like the United States and Israel. It argues that modern wars are won by the side that retains organized force after the initial blow, not the one that strikes first. Iran's strategy, formalized under IRGC General Mohammad Ali Jafari around 2005 and restructured into 32 semi-autonomous provincial commands, prevents 'decapitation' by dispersing authority so that no single strike can paralyze the system. Key components include the IRGC as the core, the Basij paramilitary for societal integration and local resistance, the conventional Artesh army for border defense, dispersed and mobile missile forces, layered air defenses (such as Bavar-373 and S-300 systems), and an external 'Axis of Resistance' network involving Hezbollah, Iraqi militias, and Yemen's Houthis co

item.title: Britney Spears Arrested in California on DUI Charges
item.description: Pop icon Britney Spears was arrested by the California Highway Patrol on Wednesday evening at approximately 9:30 pm on suspicion of driving under the influence. She was booked into custody at 3 am the following Thursday and later released. Spears is scheduled to appear in court on May 4, where the case will proceed. Official documents list her occupation as 'celebrity,' and while authorities have not released additional details about the incident, the arrest comes amid growing public concern over Spears' recent behavior and social media activity. In recent months, the singer's posts have sparked speculation among fans, and she recently disabled her Instagram account, a primary platform she used to communicate with the public. This disappearance added to the concerns over her well-being. Spears, who gained global fame in the late 1990s and early 2000s, has long had her personal life scrutinized alongside her music career. Her court appearance will provide clarity on the legal outcomes r

item.title: Middle East Conflict Raises Concerns Over Global Economic Stability
item.description: The ongoing military actions by the United States and Israel against Iran have caused a sharp rise in energy prices, prompting serious concerns about global economic stability. The British Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rachel Reeves, had intended to use her spring economic statement to convey confidence and stability, but the sudden escalation in the Middle East has disrupted these plans. The Office for Budget Responsibility has acknowledged that the conflict will have a severe impact on both the UK and global economies. Britain's economy is particularly vulnerable to rising oil and gas prices, which directly affect electricity costs. As energy prices remain high, inflation is unlikely to drop to the government's target of 2%, limiting the Bank of England's ability to lower interest rates. Additionally, government borrowing costs are rising due to higher yields on 10-year government bonds, reflecting increased military spending and budget deficits. Before the conflict, growth projection

item.title: Lily Allen References David Harbour and Breakup Themes During West End Girl Tour Performance
item.description: During her West End Girl tour stop at Glasgow Royal Concert Hall on March 2, 2026, British singer Lily Allen made a striking visual statement tied to the themes of her 2024 album. On stage, she wore a dress constructed from long pieces of green fabric printed to resemble purchase receipts allegedly linked to her former husband, actor David Harbour. The moment corresponded with the lyrics from her song “4chanStan,” which describes discovering a luxury handbag receipt possibly connected to infidelity. As part of the performance, Allen pulled the printed fabric from beside a stage bed and wrapped it around herself while singing the verse, visually portraying the emotional narrative of the song. Later, she interacted with stage props including a refrigerator, from which she retrieved another fabric printed with handwritten lyrics, using it as a head wrap to dramatize the chorus referencing 'a sad, sad man.' The performance illustrates the theatrical and autobiographical elements Allen has

item.title: Critique of Western Support for US-Israel Strikes on Iran in 2026
item.description: In this opinion article dated March 3, 2026, Dr. Binoy Kampmark argues that US allies have abandoned international law principles by endorsing or tolerating the joint US-Israeli military strikes on Iran that began on February 28, 2026. These strikes, which killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and targeted nuclear and missile facilities, are portrayed as an illegal preemptive attack violating UN Charter Article 2(4), which prohibits the threat or use of force against another state's territorial integrity. The author contrasts this with strong Western condemnation of Russia's actions in Ukraine and highlights hypocritical positions from countries like the UK, Australia, and Canada. For instance, Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong supported preventing Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons without addressing the strikes' legality, while the UK framed its involvement as 'defensive' support for regional allies. Kampmark criticizes leaders such as Canada's Mark Carney for inc

item.title: India Considers Russian Oil to Reduce Dependence on Gulf Supplies Amid Strait of Hormuz Blockade
item.description: India relies heavily on imported oil, with over half coming from the Middle East and passing through the Strait of Hormuz. The recent closure of this strategic waterway has created a potential crisis for the country, which imports 88% of its oil and has reserves that could last only about 50 days. Unlike China or Japan, India’s limited crude reserves make it highly vulnerable to supply disruptions. Indian refineries have expressed interest in restarting imports of Russian oil, which had been reduced due to U.S. pressure and punitive tariffs imposed last year. These tariffs were partially lifted following a trade agreement, but the Supreme Court later annulled the tariffs and the deal, creating uncertainty. In the meantime, refineries have also sought alternative routes, such as receiving oil from Saudi Arabia via the Red Sea, though this route is also threatened by regional instability. The Indian government now faces a critical decision: increase Russian oil imports to secure fuel sup

item.title: Former Green Bay Packers President and CEO Bob Harlan Passes Away at 89
item.description: Bob Harlan, who led the Green Bay Packers as president and CEO from 1989 to 2008, has died at the age of 89 following a brief illness. The team announced his passing on March 5, 2026, after his family informed them. Harlan had recently been hospitalized with pneumonia. During his tenure, he played a key role in revitalizing the franchise, which had struggled prior to his leadership. He hired general manager Ron Wolf, who orchestrated the trade for quarterback Brett Favre from the Atlanta Falcons, leading to consistent winning seasons from 1992 to 2004 and a Super Bowl victory in 1997. Harlan also oversaw a major $295 million renovation of Lambeau Field, preserving and modernizing the iconic stadium while ensuring the publicly owned team's long-term stability. NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell praised him for restoring the Packers' tradition of excellence and contributing to the league's success in small-market cities. Brett Favre credited Harlan's support during challenges in his career a

item.title: European Airlines See Short-Term Gains Amid Middle East Airspace Closures
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has caused significant disruptions in the region's major international air hubs, such as Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Doha. This has unintentionally benefited European airlines, as travelers are forced to seek alternative routes between Europe and Asia. Airlines including Air France, Lufthansa, KLM, and British Airways have responded by adding additional flights and increasing seating capacity. For example, Air France expanded its flights to Bangkok, Delhi, and Singapore, raising its March schedule to 908 flights to Asia compared to 800 in February. Lufthansa and KLM have also added dozens of flights, while British Airways has scheduled nearly 100 extra routes. Despite these gains, rising oil prices remain a concern. Jet fuel costs have surged by 25% within just four days, affecting overall operational expenses. European airlines have mitigated part of this impact through fuel surcharges, which add roughly $32 per passenger on long-haul flights like New

item.title: California’s Sierra Nevada Snowpack Declines Rapidly Due to Early Spring Heat Wave
item.description: In late February and early March 2026, California experienced a dramatic reduction in its Sierra Nevada snowpack following an intense winter heat wave. NASA satellite imagery reveals that snow levels that had accumulated after major storms in mid-February diminished substantially in less than ten days, with temperatures soaring 15 to 20 degrees above normal. According to UC Agriculture and Natural Resources climate scientist Daniel Swain, the speed of the snow loss is extraordinary. The northern Sierra currently holds only 38% of its average snowpack, while the southern Sierra is at 83%, a notable drop from earlier levels near 98%. The reduced snowpack has significant implications for California, which relies on it for roughly 30% of its water supply. Experts attribute the trend to climate change, as warmer winters increasingly cause precipitation to fall as rain rather than snow and shift snowlines higher in elevation. Continued above-average temperatures may further diminish snow res

item.title: Israeli Government Faces Lawsuits from Contractors and Influencers Over Unpaid Propaganda Work During Gaza Conflict
item.description: Israel's international propaganda apparatus, managed through the Prime Minister's Office after the collapse of the Ministry of Information following the October 2023 events, is encountering significant legal challenges. Influencers, consultants, and private companies recruited to promote pro-Israel narratives abroad during the Gaza war have filed lawsuits claiming millions of shekels in unpaid fees. According to reports from Israeli outlet Calcalist, the government bypassed standard tender processes by expanding existing private contracts to channel payments, but many invoices remain unsettled. For instance, Intellect Production and Publishing Group sued for about 1.7 million shekels (around $552,000) related to travel and operations countering pro-Palestine protests at the International Court of Justice. Former government spokesperson Eylon Levy stated he is owed money, with his monthly salary of 41,125 shekels routed through such a company rather than paid directly. Another firm, Spe

item.title: Media Focus on Sexual Scandals vs. Global War Crimes
item.description: The article critiques the disproportionate attention given by the media to alleged sexual misconduct by public figures such as Trump, particularly regarding underage individuals, while largely ignoring the far more severe consequences of geopolitical actions, including wars and military interventions led by the United States. The author argues that these interventions have caused millions of deaths over the past decades, citing bombings, invasions, coups, and sanctions. The piece emphasizes that while sexual misconduct is morally and legally condemnable, it pales in comparison to the large-scale human suffering caused by international conflicts and imperial policies. The author also criticizes the media for selectively framing crimes, presenting minor offenses as sensational and obscuring systemic violence driven by state powers. Additionally, the article touches on current humanitarian crises, such as the impact of sanctions on Cuba, advocating for international cooperation among Span

item.title: Impact of the Strait of Hormuz Closure on Global Oil Markets
item.description: Following attacks on Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, approximately one-fifth of the world's oil supply has been removed from the market, causing a sharp rise in prices. Brent crude is currently trading 10% higher over-the-counter compared to last Friday, with futures expected to surpass $80 per barrel and potentially exceed the psychological $100 mark if the situation persists. The closure significantly affects Asian imports: about half of India's and China's crude oil and 80% of Japan's pass through Hormuz, with limited alternatives via pipelines to the Red Sea. Should the Houthi militias close Bab el-Mandeb in solidarity with Iran, the disruption could be catastrophic. While the attacks may permanently reduce Iran's production by around 5%, Russia emerges as the only major supplier able to fill the gap, benefiting economically from the price surge despite long-standing sanctions. China, having built up reserves during lower prices, maintains strategic flexibility. The U

item.title: A Practical Guide to Selecting a Home That Fits Your Daily Life
item.description: Choosing a home is one of the most significant decisions a person can make, both financially and personally. The process should go beyond visual appeal or price, focusing instead on how the property aligns with daily routines, lifestyle needs, and long-term plans. Buyers should start by evaluating their lifestyle, including work habits, entertaining preferences, and time spent at home. Location is equally crucial, as it affects convenience, safety, commute times, and access to amenities. When comparing properties, attention should be given to layout, natural lighting, storage, and overall functionality rather than just size. Long-term considerations, such as potential family growth, remote work needs, and accessibility, ensure the home remains suitable over time. Establishing a realistic budget that includes not only the purchase price but also taxes, insurance, maintenance, and utilities is essential for financial stability. Additionally, the neighborhood environment impacts daily lif

item.title: Bianca Censori Attends Court Hearing for Kanye West Lawsuit in Modest Attire
item.description: Bianca Censori, known for her bold fashion choices, made headlines for a notably restrained appearance at a Los Angeles courthouse while supporting her husband, Kanye West. Opting for a modest dress and a neat bun, Censori presented a calm and focused presence during the ongoing civil lawsuit filed by Tony Saxon. Saxon, who previously worked for West as a project manager on his Malibu mansion renovations, claims he was not properly compensated, faced unsafe working conditions, and was wrongfully terminated. Court documents indicate that Saxon sometimes worked up to 16-hour days while managing the project and raised safety concerns, including a proposal to move large generators indoors, which he warned against. According to the lawsuit, tensions escalated, and West allegedly threatened Saxon regarding his compliance. West has denied all allegations, with his legal team arguing the claims are baseless and seeking dismissal of the case. Inside the courtroom, a sketch by Mona Shafer Edward

item.title: ExpressVPN Partners with Internet Watch Foundation to Block Known Child Sexual Abuse Material Domains
item.description: ExpressVPN, a prominent virtual private network provider, has announced a partnership with the Internet Watch Foundation (IWF), the UK's leading organization combating child sexual abuse material (CSAM) online. This initiative makes ExpressVPN one of the first major VPN services to actively restrict access to websites dedicated solely to hosting verified CSAM, using a straightforward DNS-level blocking mechanism derived from the IWF's trusted list of such domains. According to Dr. Peter Membrey, ExpressVPN's chief engineering officer, the approach prioritizes user privacy by avoiding any encryption breakage, traffic inspection, or user monitoring, ensuring transparency and maintaining trust in privacy-focused services. The company describes the move as an ideological step rather than a complex technical innovation, highlighting the courage required to be among the first in the industry to take a stand against content universally condemned as criminal and harmful. The IWF has praised th

item.title: Life in Utqiagvik, Alaska: Challenges and Traditions at the Northern Edge of the U.S.
item.description: Utqiagvik, formerly known as Barrow, is the northernmost inhabited town in the United States, located at 71 degrees north in Alaska. With more than five thousand residents, the town faces extreme living conditions, including seventy days of continuous darkness during winter, temperatures below zero for nine months of the year, and high costs for basic goods due to the town’s isolation. Barrow has no road connections; supplies arrive mainly via subsidized flights from Anchorage, with occasional summer barges bringing larger goods. Public sector employment dominates, while jobs such as taxi driving are highly valued due to extreme cold. The town’s daily life adapts to harsh conditions, using vitamin D and specialized lighting during dark months, and taking advantage of the long summer days when the sun does not set for 81 consecutive days. Despite these challenges, the town maintains a strong sense of community, tradition, and cultural continuity as a historical Iñupiat hunting and fishi

item.title: NASA Updates Artemis Program: First Crewed Moon Landing Shifted to Artemis IV in 2028, New 2027 Orbital Test Mission Added
item.description: NASA, under new Administrator Jared Isaacman, has announced significant revisions to the Artemis lunar program to increase mission cadence, reduce risks, and maintain the goal of a U.S. crewed Moon landing in 2028. The key change shifts the first crewed lunar landing from the originally planned Artemis III to Artemis IV, while introducing a new Artemis III mission in 2027. This new mission will be a crewed test in low Earth orbit (LEO), where the SLS rocket and Orion spacecraft will rendezvous and dock with one or both Human Landing System (HLS) vehicles: SpaceX's Starship-based Moonship or Blue Origin's Blue Moon Mark 2. The LEO test will validate docking procedures, propellant transfer concepts (if applicable), the Axiom AxEMU spacesuit (potentially without an EVA), and overall integration, easing pressure on providers by avoiding immediate lunar demonstrations of complex orbital refueling. The timeline remains ambitious, with Artemis IV targeted for 2028 as the landing mission, and

item.title: Geopolitical Risks of U.S. and Israeli Actions Against Iran
item.description: The article analyzes the escalating geopolitical tensions caused by U.S. and Israeli actions in Iran, highlighting the potential regional and global consequences. The author argues that the Trump administration has abandoned international law, relying instead on military force to achieve strategic objectives. The assassination of key Iranian figures and previous interventions in Venezuela, Ukraine, and elsewhere are framed as part of a broader strategy to maintain Western hegemony and counter the rise of China. The piece criticizes the United Nations and European Union for their impotence or complicity, noting that their institutions were designed for a different world order and now fail to check aggression. Iran, despite being militarily strong and strategically vital, faces a combination of internal pressures and external threats. The article also underscores that the nuclear issue is secondary to geopolitical aims, and emphasizes that any military escalation could have serious conse

item.title: Toronto Studio Designs Eco-Friendly and Artistic Balloon Bouquets
item.description: Toronto-based studio Ballooms, led by founder Dylan Kwacz, is redefining the concept of balloon arrangements by creating eco-friendly, visually striking bouquets made entirely from biodegradable latex. Moving beyond traditional birthday balloons, these oversized and surreal floral-inspired designs offer a playful yet sophisticated aesthetic, suitable for both personal gifts and large-scale installations. Prices for ready-made bouquets start around $45 CAD, with more elaborate or metallic options ranging from $50 to $75. Ballooms also collaborates with brands and venues such as Lululemon, The Power Plant, and Aritzia, extending its designs to fashion launches, gallery exhibitions, and other events. By emphasizing sustainability and high design, Ballooms transforms a simple balloon into an artistic and environmentally conscious experience, expanding both the scale and imagination of what balloons can represent.

item.title: Queimada Grande: Facts About Brazil’s Restricted Snake Island
item.description: Queimada Grande, often sensationalized as 'the deadliest island on Earth,' is a small island off the coast of São Paulo, Brazil, known for its endemic Golden Lancehead snakes (Bothrops insularis). These highly venomous snakes have existed on the island for thousands of years due to its isolation after the last glaciation. Contrary to exaggerated reports, the island is home to only a few thousand snakes, far from claims of one per square meter. The island is uninhabited, with a lighthouse that was staffed until the early 20th century, and visiting is strictly restricted to scientists and researchers. Popular media often misrepresents the danger, claiming that humans would not survive even thirty seconds on the island, yet in reality, no fatalities or human bites have been documented. Recent expeditions, including a well-publicized visit by YouTubers like MrBeast and Drew Binski, confirm that encounters with snakes are rare and controlled, with only a handful seen over several hours. The

item.title: Understanding Common Congenital Heart Diseases and Their Development
item.description: The human heart is one of the earliest and most vital organs to develop in an embryo, beginning to beat just four weeks after fertilization to supply nutrients and oxygen necessary for survival. Any disruptions during early cardiac development can result in congenital heart diseases (CHDs). This article explains the formation of the heart from the embryonic mesoderm, focusing on the lateral visceral layer, which forms the cardiovascular system. Initially, two groups of cells form a horseshoe-shaped cardiogenic field that eventually fuses into the primitive heart tube, which gives rise to key heart structures such as the atria, ventricles, and major arteries. The article highlights three common CHDs: tricuspid atresia, tetralogy of Fallot, and patent ductus arteriosus (PDA). Tricuspid atresia occurs when the tricuspid valve fails to develop properly, leading to underdeveloped right ventricles and abnormal blood flow that often requires early medical intervention. Tetralogy of Fallot com

item.title: Revealing the 23-F Documents: The Role of the King, Armada, and Civil-Military Plots
item.description: On February 25, 2026, the Spanish government released 153 official files and 89 files from Summary 2/81 regarding the 1981 23-F coup attempt. The documents reveal that King Juan Carlos actively opposed the coup, issuing direct orders to prevent General Alfonso Armada from entering Zarzuela Palace and denouncing the plotters' plans. The papers confirm that some coup leaders misused the King's name to sway undecided military officers, while others, like Tejero, were unaware of the broader political manipulations. Armada had drafted a 'government of national concentration,' which included both military figures and democratic politicians like Felipe González, but his plan faced rejection from the coup enforcers. Detailed intelligence reports also show that U.S. and Vatican officials monitored the situation closely, weighing Spain's strategic and moral alignment. Additionally, newly declassified transcripts highlight the chaotic and surreal behavior of key participants, including logistical

item.title: Baikonur’s Area 31 Launch Pad Restored for Manned and Cargo Soyuz Flights
item.description: Russia has restored the only launch pad at Baikonur capable of sending manned Soyuz spacecraft and Progress cargo ships to the International Space Station (ISS). On November 27, 2025, Soyuz MS-28 launched successfully, but the service platform under the rocket fell into the ramp pit, destroying the 17-ton, 19-meter-long platform designated 8U216. This platform is essential for launch preparations and maintenance, as it moves on rails and locks in place with chains during liftoff. Roscosmos initially postponed the Progress MS-33 launch, with expectations of completing repairs by spring 2026. Work began in December, with over 150 engineers from Roscosmos and TsENKI working in two shifts to dismantle and repair the service platform. The outage of this pad impacted not only Russia but all ISS partners, including the United States, Japan, Canada, and ESA, since Soyuz and Progress spacecraft perform critical crew transport and orbital maintenance tasks. While other Soyuz pads exist at Pleset

item.title: Sustainable Paper Tube Chair Combines Upcycling and Minimalist Design
item.description: The Paper Tube Chair, created by the Dhammada Collective in Chandigarh, India, transforms industrial paper cores into a functional and aesthetically appealing piece of furniture. Made from 15 discarded heavy-duty paper tubes and red rope, the chair demonstrates how upcycled materials can achieve both durability and high-end minimalist design. Its construction relies on a simple yet strong 'figure-eight' rope lashing that tightens under weight, while 3D-printed bases protect floor contact points. The tubes are varnished to improve longevity, yet retain their original industrial markings, celebrating their previous use. This project addresses a recycling gap, as industrial paper cores often cannot be processed locally due to adhesive content. By upcycling them into furniture, the chair prevents waste from ending up in landfills and gives materials a second life as design objects. The open-source design allows easy replication, repair, and maintenance, and its modular nature ensures indiv

item.title: Rep. Tony Gonzales to Complete Current Term but Will Not Run for Re-election Following Ethics Probe
item.description: Rep. Tony Gonzales, a Republican from Texas, announced that he will not seek re-election following revelations of an extramarital affair with a former staffer and an ensuing House Ethics Committee investigation. Gonzales, a married father of six, admitted to the affair during a conservative talk radio appearance, one day after advancing to a GOP primary runoff. In his statement, Gonzales emphasized his commitment to serving his constituents and his country, highlighting his two decades of military service and three terms in Congress. The House Ethics Committee is examining whether Gonzales engaged in sexual misconduct and whether he granted any special favors or privileges as a result of the affair. Despite the controversy, Gonzales intends to complete his current term, continuing his legislative duties and representation of his district. The announcement comes amid increasing scrutiny of political conduct and personal accountability among public officials, reflecting broader debates a

item.title: Anthropic Plans Legal Challenge Against DOD Supply-Chain Risk Designation
item.description: Anthropic, the AI company founded by Dario Amodei, has announced plans to challenge the U.S. Department of Defense's (DOD) decision to label it a supply-chain risk. This designation could prevent the company from working with the Pentagon and its contractors. Amodei described the label as “legally unsound” and emphasized that Anthropic’s AI systems will not be used for mass surveillance of Americans or fully autonomous weapons, in contrast to the Pentagon’s desire for unrestricted access. The supply-chain risk label, according to Amodei, is narrowly targeted, affecting only Anthropic’s AI use in direct Department of War contracts, leaving most customers unaffected. The announcement follows a leaked internal memo in which Amodei criticized OpenAI’s relationship with the DOD, calling it “safety theater.” OpenAI has already signed a deal to take Anthropic’s place in working with the Department, which has caused internal backlash. While Anthropic continues to support U.S. military operatio

item.title: Russell Wilson Reiterates Criticism of Sean Payton as 'Classless' in Recent Podcast Interview
item.description: Russell Wilson, the veteran NFL quarterback and free agent following his 2025 stint with the New York Giants, has reignited his longstanding tension with Denver Broncos head coach Sean Payton. In a resurfaced interview on the 'Bussin’ With the Boys' podcast, recorded before the most recent Super Bowl, Wilson addressed his October 2025 social media response where he called Payton 'classless' and referenced the coach's past involvement in the New Orleans Saints' bounty scandal. The comments stemmed from Payton's postgame remarks after a Broncos win over the Giants, where he praised rookie quarterback Jaxson Dart's 'spark' and suggested the game might have been easier against Wilson, whom he implied should have remained the starter longer. Wilson interpreted this as an unnecessary slight, especially since he was no longer with the Broncos after a difficult 2023 season under Payton that ended with him being benched and released. Wilson emphasized his respect for Payton as a play-caller but

item.title: HuffPost Launches 'Voices of Disabled People' Section to Amplify Authentic Disability Narratives
item.description: HuffPost has launched a new section called 'Voices of Disabled People,' dedicated to sharing authentic stories from disabled individuals and communities. In a letter from Rajul Punjabi-Johnson, Director of HuffPost Voices, the launch is introduced with a personal anecdote about a close friend who experienced a stroke at age 23, resulting in locked-in syndrome. Despite severe challenges, she regained much of her life through intensive rehabilitation and adapted by starting a blog using eye-tracking technology, eventually pursuing an MFA and writing a book. The editor emphasizes that storytelling allows people to own their narratives, resist limitations, and view disability as one aspect of identity rather than a defect needing repair. The section avoids 'inspiration porn,' tokenism, or sensationalism, instead focusing on honest, timely, and intersectional perspectives that challenge ableism and celebrate joy and truth. With more than 1 in 4 Americans living with a disability, the initia

item.title: Desalination Plants in the Persian Gulf Face Risk Amid Escalating Regional Conflict
item.description: Countries in the Persian Gulf, including Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, and Qatar, heavily rely on desalination plants powered by fossil fuels to provide drinking water for millions of people. These plants are critical infrastructure, supplying roughly 60% of global desalinated water, with key complexes in Dubai, Fujairah, and Kuwait already affected by nearby military attacks. The ongoing war that began on February 28, 2026, with U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran, has seen retaliatory attacks by Iranian forces on oil and gas facilities. Some of these strikes have caused collateral damage near desalination plants, threatening water security in the region. Historical precedents, such as Iraq's sabotage during the 1991 Gulf War, highlight the vulnerability of these facilities. More recent threats came from Houthi drone and missile attacks on Saudi plants in 2019 and 2022, although they caused minimal damage. Experts warn that Iran’s more advanced arsenal could inflict sig

item.title: Heated Rivalry Fandom Highlights Platform Differences in Online Discourse
item.description: The WIRED article examines why discussions around the hit series Heated Rivalry—a queer romance between two closeted hockey players—feel particularly awkward and divisive online. On Tumblr, the show has revitalized fandom spaces, bringing back longtime users who appreciate open conversations about attraction, desire, and slash fanfiction in a supportive environment. In contrast, X (formerly Twitter) hosts more toxic infighting, amplified by its algorithm that promotes outrage and the platform's rightward shift under Elon Musk. This has driven some fans back to Tumblr or alternatives like Threads. The piece references backlash to a Vulture article that explored the show's popularity among women and 'fujoshi' culture, noting that many critics reacted based on headlines without reading the largely sympathetic piece. Experts point to historical factors like Tumblr's 2018 porn ban, which scattered fandoms, and uneven content moderation across platforms. Ultimately, the article suggests that

item.title: Iran's Succession Process After Khamenei's Death: Interim Council Formed and Potential Candidates Emerge Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: Following the assassination of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on February 28, 2026, in joint US-Israeli strikes, Iran initiated its second supreme leader transition in the Islamic Republic's history. An Interim Leadership Council, comprising President Masoud Pezeshkian, Chief Justice Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i, and Ayatollah Alireza Arafi, was established on March 1 under Article 111 of the Constitution to handle supreme leader duties temporarily. The Assembly of Experts, an 88-member body of clerics, is tasked with electing the new leader swiftly, especially given the ongoing war, to demonstrate regime stability to domestic and foreign adversaries. An Israeli bombing targeted the assembly's Qom building on March 3, though no casualties occurred after evacuation. Potential candidates include Mojtaba Khamenei, Khamenei's son, despite his mid-ranking clerical status and lack of public office experience; the late leader opposed hereditary succession to avoid dynastic perceptions antithetical to

item.title: UK Nuclear Submarine Base Risks and Global Nuclear Arms Escalation
item.description: The article examines growing nuclear threats through the lens of Britain's Devonport dockyard in Plymouth, the UK's primary site for maintaining, refueling, and decommissioning nuclear-powered submarines. Operated by Babcock International, the facility has seen significant profits from nuclear-related work, yet surrounding communities remain economically disadvantaged. Twelve of 16 decommissioned submarines there still hold nuclear fuel, creating an on-site waste stockpile. At least 10 major radioactive leaks have occurred over 30 years, with contaminants like plutonium, americium, and tritium detected along the nearby coastline and wildlife areas. Former workers report health issues such as cancer, linked to exposure, amid a pervasive culture of secrecy. Local preparations include stockpiles of iodine tablets for potential radiation incidents in the residential zone. The piece ties this to broader UK investment in nuclear capabilities—£4.4 billion planned over a decade—and high milita

item.title: Sophisticated iOS Exploit Kit 'Coruna' Proliferates from Suspected US Origins to Russian Spies and Cybercriminals
item.description: Security researchers from Google and iVerify have uncovered 'Coruna,' a highly advanced iPhone hacking toolkit that exploits 23 vulnerabilities in iOS versions 13 through 17.2.1, enabling zero-click malware installation via malicious websites. The kit includes five full exploit chains and bypasses multiple iOS defenses. Google first detected elements in February 2025 linked to a surveillance company customer, followed by its use in a suspected Russian espionage campaign against Ukrainian websites in July 2025. By late 2025, it appeared in a profit-driven operation targeting Chinese-language crypto and gambling sites to steal cryptocurrency, photos, and emails. iVerify's analysis suggests Coruna originated from or resembles tools developed for the US government, citing code similarities to the 2023 'Triangulation' operation (attributed by Russia to the NSA), professional English-language authorship, and modular design indicative of nation-state investment costing millions. This prolifer

item.title: Ex-Girardi Keese Attorney Keith Griffin Pleads Guilty to Criminal Contempt in Lion Air Settlement Funds Case
item.description: Keith Griffin, a 54-year-old former lawyer at the now-defunct Girardi Keese firm, pleaded guilty on March 5, 2026, to one count of criminal contempt in federal court in Chicago. He admitted to disobeying a court order from U.S. District Judge Thomas M. Durkin to promptly distribute $7.5 million in settlement funds from Boeing to the families of victims killed in the 2018 Lion Air Flight 610 crash in Indonesia, which claimed all 189 lives. The funds were received in March 2020, but Griffin acknowledged that the firm withheld full distribution for eight months despite client inquiries and his own confrontations with Tom Girardi. Griffin also admitted to withholding information from a co-counsel assisting in the case. The victims ultimately received their payments from another firm's insurer. This case stems from the broader scandal involving Tom Girardi, once a prominent trial lawyer known for his lavish lifestyle, who was disbarred in 2022 and convicted in 2024 of embezzling millions fr

item.title: Australian Greens Condemn US-Israeli Strikes on Iran and Criticize Labor Government's Support
item.description: The Australian Greens have strongly opposed the recent joint US-Israeli military strikes on Iran, which began in late February 2026 under President Donald Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. These actions, described by the Greens as illegal breaches of international law and wars of aggression, have caused significant chaos in the Middle East, including reported civilian deaths such as at least 60 children in strikes on a school in southern Iran. The Greens condemned the Iranian regime's oppression and supported the Woman Life Freedom movement but argued that US-led bombing will not liberate Iranians, instead killing innocents, destroying infrastructure, and deepening poverty and fear. They highlighted the likelihood of Australian involvement through joint US-Australian facilities like Pine Gap and North West Cape, which may provide intelligence or targeting assistance. Greens Leader Senator Larissa Waters called the attacks abhorrent and unilateral, urging the Albanese Labor g

item.title: A Historical Timeline Contextualizing the Ukraine War
item.description: This article provides a detailed historical timeline explaining the Ukraine war, emphasizing the geopolitical context often ignored by mainstream media. It traces tensions back decades, highlighting the role of Ukrainian nationalist movements, U.S. and NATO policies, and Russian security concerns. Key points include the involvement of Ukrainian fascists during and after WWII, U.S. support for anti-Soviet elements, and NATO expansion eastward despite warnings from Russian and U.S. officials about potential consequences. The article covers major milestones, including the 2004 Orange Revolution, the 2014 U.S.-backed coup in Ukraine, Crimea's referendum to join Russia, and the Donbass conflict. It also outlines failed peace efforts, such as the Minsk accords, and subsequent escalation of military interventions by both sides. The narrative frames Russia’s actions as defensive responses to Western provocations, while portraying U.S. and NATO policies as aggressive and destabilizing. The time

item.title: AI is being used to assist US military operations in Iran, raising concerns about human oversight
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly integrated into modern warfare, providing militaries with the ability to process massive amounts of data to flag targets, rank threats, and suggest operational priorities. In conflicts such as Gaza and the reported US operation in Iran, AI systems like Anthropic's Claude have been employed to enhance decision-making and improve operational efficiency. Experts emphasize that while AI does not autonomously launch attacks, it acts as a persuasive decision-support tool that can influence human commanders under high-pressure, fast-paced scenarios. This reliance raises concerns about the erosion of effective human oversight, potentially reducing critical engagement to a 'rubber-stamping' process. AI's statistical approach to data can be effective in predictable situations but can fail in complex, unpredictable environments, as demonstrated by errors in basic tasks. The US Department of War has explicitly pushed for an 'AI-first' military approach,

item.title: Britney Spears detained overnight in California on suspicion of DUI
item.description: Pop star Britney Spears was taken into custody by California highway patrol officers late Wednesday night and spent several hours in detention before being released early Thursday morning. According to the Ventura County Sheriff's Office, Spears was arrested at 9:28 p.m. and released at 6:07 a.m., and her court appearance is scheduled for May 4. Media reports indicate she was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence. Her representative described the incident as 'completely inexcusable' but suggested it could be a catalyst for long-overdue changes in her life, emphasizing the need for support from her loved ones and a plan for her well-being. Spears, who had previously fought a highly publicized legal battle to end the conservatorship controlled by her father, has been largely inactive in the music industry for several years. Despite her hiatus, her earlier hits remain popular, and she recently signed a major deal to sell the rights to her music catalog. The Ventura County a

item.title: Trump Removes Kristi Noem as Homeland Security Secretary, Names Sen. Markwayne Mullin as Replacement
item.description: On March 5, 2026, President Donald Trump announced via Truth Social that Kristi Noem was being replaced as Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security, effective March 31, 2026. Trump nominated U.S. Senator Markwayne Mullin from Oklahoma to take over the role, praising Noem's service and border security achievements while reassigning her to a new position as Special Envoy for 'The Shield of the Americas,' a forthcoming Western Hemisphere security initiative. Despite Trump's public commendation, a White House official described Noem's dismissal to NBC News as the 'culmination of her many unfortunate leadership failures,' citing issues such as fallout from incidents in Minnesota where U.S. citizens were killed by ICE and Border Patrol agents—events Noem controversially labeled involving 'domestic terrorism'—a costly $220 million ad campaign promoting voluntary self-deportation that she claimed had Trump's prior approval but which he denied, slow FEMA emergency funding responses, sta

item.title: UK Diesel Prices Reach 16-Month High Amid Rising Oil Costs from Middle East Conflict
item.description: UK diesel prices have climbed to a 16-month high of 147 pence per liter, while petrol has risen to 136 pence per liter, according to the RAC. This surge follows the outbreak of war in the Middle East, triggered by US and Israeli military strikes on Iran, prompting Iranian retaliatory attacks on Gulf nations including the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and others. These attacks have severely disrupted oil and natural gas production and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global energy supplies where over 80 tankers normally pass daily. Shipping has slowed dramatically due to threats and attacks, causing wholesale fuel prices in the UK to jump significantly—diesel by 7 pence per liter and petrol by 2 pence initially, with further increases expected. The Petrol Retailers' Association (PRA) emphasized that retailers are passing on genuine wholesale cost increases rather than profiteering, advising drivers to compare prices locally. Brent crude oil rose over 5% to ar

item.title: Maxim Shkret's Colorful and Expressive Animal Illustrations
item.description: Digital artist Maxim Shkret has created a captivating collection of animal portraits characterized by bold colors, dynamic compositions, and a unique futuristic style. His work spans a wide range of creatures, from polar bears to hummingbirds, and even imaginative five-pointed unicorns. Each portrait is meticulously crafted without the use of AI, emphasizing Shkret's manual artistic skill and attention to detail. The illustrations stand out for their vibrant color combinations and dramatic yet inviting aesthetic, capturing both the energy and personality of the animals. Shkret's approach transforms traditional animal portraiture into a maximalist, expressive experience, inviting viewers to engage with wildlife in a visually stimulating way. The artist shares his work on platforms like Instagram, where the vivid and playful style has garnered significant attention from art enthusiasts. Moss and Fog highlights Shkret's ability to blend artistry and imagination, offering a fresh perspecti

item.title: New Analysis Shows Tyrannosaurus rex Grew Over Four Decades
item.description: A new study challenges previous assumptions about the growth of Tyrannosaurus rex, suggesting that this iconic dinosaur took approximately 40 years to reach its full size, rather than the earlier estimate of 25 years. By analyzing growth rings in the leg bones of 17 tyrannosaur specimens, researchers used advanced statistical modeling and specialized lighting techniques to detect previously overlooked growth markers. This comprehensive approach allowed them to reconstruct a more detailed life history of T. rex, showing a prolonged growth period rather than rapid maturation. The study also raises questions about the classification of some fossils, suggesting that certain specimens, including the well-known 'Jane' and 'Petey,' might represent different species or subspecies within the Tyrannosaurus complex. The findings imply that T. rex's extended development may have allowed juveniles to occupy different ecological roles, contributing to the species' dominance as apex predators at the

item.title: Countries Affected by the Ongoing Conflict Involving Iran
item.description: Since February 28, 2026, a series of military strikes by the United States and Israel against Iran has triggered a regional escalation affecting over a dozen Middle Eastern countries. Iran has retaliated with drone and missile attacks targeting Israel, US embassies, military bases, and key infrastructure across neighboring nations. Casualties have exceeded a thousand in the region, with damage to schools, hospitals, airports, ports, luxury hotels, and military facilities. Countries directly impacted include Israel, Iran, Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Cyprus, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Syria, Turkey, and the United Arab Emirates. The conflict has disrupted global supply chains, particularly in oil, gas, and fertilizer production, and halted shipping along the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Some countries, including the US, UK, Germany, Ireland, and Italy, have begun evacuating citizens due to safety concerns. Although the Trump administration justifies the military op

item.title: Ocean Patterns Limit Global Drought Spread and Protect Food Security
item.description: Researchers at the Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar (IITGN), collaborating with international partners, have found that ocean temperature patterns significantly influence the spread of droughts across the globe. Analyzing over a century of climate data (1901–2020), the team discovered that synchronized droughts rarely affect more than 1.8%–6.5% of global land at the same time, which is much lower than previous estimates suggesting up to one-sixth of the planet could face simultaneous drought. This limited spread is largely driven by ocean cycles such as El Niño and La Niña, which create a patchwork of regional drought conditions rather than a single, planet-wide dry spell. The study identifies recurring 'drought hubs' including Australia, South America, southern Africa, and parts of North America. While moderate droughts can increase crop failure risks—sometimes exceeding 40% for maize and soybeans—natural oceanic variability helps mitigate the risk of simultaneous global cro

item.title: Nida Allam Concedes Narrow Loss to Valerie Foushee in North Carolina Democratic Primary
item.description: Durham County Commissioner Nida Allam conceded the Democratic primary for North Carolina's 4th Congressional District after a closely contested race against incumbent Rep. Valerie Foushee. Allam, 32, emphasized her progressive platform, which included labeling the Gaza conflict as genocide, abolishing ICE, and ending U.S. involvement in wars abroad. She criticized the Democratic establishment for aligning with corporate Super PACs and warmongering lobbies and highlighted that her campaign drove a significant increase in voter turnout, particularly among young voters. Foushee, who received endorsements from Gov. Josh Stein and former Gov. Roy Cooper, narrowly won with 49.2% of the vote compared to Allam's 48.2%, according to NBC News. Despite the loss, Allam framed the campaign as a milestone for representation, especially for young, Muslim, immigrant women in politics. Her campaign was supported by progressive groups such as Justice Democrats, Leaders We Deserve, Sunrise Movement, Indi

item.title: GLP-1 medications may help reduce heart tissue damage after heart attacks, study finds
item.description: A recent study published in Nature Communications by the University of Bristol and University College London suggests that GLP-1 drugs, such as semaglutide, commonly used for type 2 diabetes and obesity, may reduce the risk of serious heart complications after heart attacks. The research identifies a brain-gut-heart signaling pathway that allows these medications to open potassium channels in pericytes, tiny cells that control the dilation of microscopic blood vessels in the heart. This process can improve blood flow to heart tissue that remains blocked after standard emergency treatments, a condition known as 'no-reflow,' which affects nearly half of heart attack patients and significantly increases the risk of heart failure and death within a year. While animal models and cellular imaging demonstrated this mechanism, the study notes that clinical trials are required to confirm if the same benefits occur in humans. Additionally, the research focuses on the drugs' immediate protective

item.title: Kristi Noem Removed as Homeland Security Secretary Amid Controversial Policies
item.description: Kristi Noem has been removed from her role as Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security after a tenure marked by intense scrutiny over aggressive immigration enforcement and controversial federal operations. President Donald Trump announced her replacement by Senator Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma, a loyal ally and immigration hardliner, while Noem will transition to a newly created role as Special Envoy for The Shield of the Americas. During her time at DHS, Noem prioritized mass arrests and deportations, clashing with other administration officials over enforcement strategies. Her leadership has been criticized for policies allowing ICE to enter private homes without warrants, the deaths of U.S. citizens during federal operations in Minneapolis, and mass detention camps. Additionally, her micromanagement of FEMA contracts caused significant backlogs, affecting disaster response capabilities. Her tenure also included a $38 billion plan to expand detention facilities nationwide. A

item.title: Kaitlan Collins Defends CNN Coverage of Fallen U.S. Troops After White House Clash
item.description: CNN correspondent Kaitlan Collins responded to a tense exchange with White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt over coverage of the U.S. military's casualties in Iran. The conflict arose after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth criticized news outlets, accusing them of attempting to make President Donald Trump look bad. Collins clarified on her show 'The Source' that the network's reporting focuses on honoring the Americans who lost their lives, not on the president or the network itself. She listed the names of the fallen service members, emphasizing their sacrifice. During the briefing, Leavitt insisted that the media should highlight the successes of Operation Epic Fury and criticized Collins and CNN for allegedly negative coverage of President Trump. Collins maintained that her reporting is intended to honor the troops and their families, highlighting the human cost of the conflict. The exchange reflects ongoing tension between the administration and the press over how military oper

item.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4 With Integrated Excel and Google Sheets Functionality
item.description: OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.4, an upgraded version of its ChatGPT model, designed to improve efficiency, accuracy, and cost-effectiveness for workplace tasks. This new model allows users to work directly within Excel and Google Sheets, enabling faster and more reliable creation of files and problem-solving. According to OpenAI, GPT-5.4 outperforms human office workers on 83% of the tasks evaluated through GDPval, a benchmark measuring real-world performance across 44 different occupations. The model is optimized to use fewer tokens, which helps deliver faster responses while reducing operational costs. Alongside GPT-5.4, OpenAI launched OpenAI for Financial Services, a suite of tools that includes spreadsheet integration and specialized apps and skills within ChatGPT.
