This notification overload not only disrupts users’ experiences but also exposes Instagram’s infrastruc

item.title: SpaceX and Blue Origin Re-focus on Lunar Development Amid Pentagon's Golden Dome Initiative
item.description: In a surprising turn of events, SpaceX and Blue Origin have dramatically changed their space exploration priorities, shifting focus from Mars to the Moon. This change comes just as the Pentagon accelerates plans for a next-generation missile defense system known as the Golden Dome. Elon Musk’s SpaceX, which had previously dismissed lunar exploration, now aims to establish a permanent presence on the Moon, while Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin pauses its space tourism program to redirect resources toward lunar missions. The push to return to the Moon is tied to national defense, with the Golden Dome project aiming to create a missile shield that could be supported by space-based infrastructure. SpaceX is rumored to be in line for a significant Pentagon contract to build a satellite constellation for the defense system. Blue Origin’s Blue Ring vehicle and Blue Moon landers are also key components of this new strategy, enabling lunar logistics and sensor deployment. These developments signal a sh

item.title: Researchers combine caffeine with CRISPR for potential cancer and diabetes treatments
item.description: Researchers at Texas A&M University have developed a groundbreaking method that combines caffeine with CRISPR gene editing technology. This approach, known as chemogenetics, allows scientists to precisely control gene-editing activities within cells, turning them on or off with caffeine intake. By ingesting a small dose of caffeine, such as from coffee, chocolate, or soda, CRISPR can be activated to modify genes inside targeted cells, including immune T cells that fight cancer. The method provides an innovative way to manipulate gene expression and immune responses with minimal side effects, offering a new potential avenue for treating diseases like cancer and diabetes. Additionally, the researchers have discovered that certain drugs can reverse the process, allowing for more controlled gene therapy that could be paused and restarted depending on a patient's needs. This approach leverages common, familiar substances, such as caffeine and rapamycin, as 'control signals,' making it a pro

item.title: The Bondi Beach Incident and Its Political Impact on Gaza's Ceasefire Timeline
item.description: The violent incident on Bondi Beach in Australia came at a critical time, coinciding with Israel's diplomatic efforts on Gaza's ceasefire. With a tense situation regarding the Gaza ceasefire, Israel was under pressure to finalize key decisions before a high-stakes meeting between Prime Minister Netanyahu and President Trump. The Bondi Beach violence quickly shifted the political narrative, moving the focus from Gaza to broader issues of security, antisemitism, and emergency responses. The incident’s timing was seen as a catalyst that altered the international conversation surrounding Gaza, as it pushed issues of security and antisemitism to the forefront. Meanwhile, Israeli officials raised alarms about Iranian involvement in the violence, a claim that fit into an existing geopolitical framework already aligned with Israel’s interests. The article explores how such events can have far-reaching political consequences, reshaping diplomatic strategies and altering the timelines of critica

item.title: New Study Challenges Theory About Antarctic Glaciers' Role in Climate Change
item.description: A popular theory about Antarctic glaciers, which suggested that their melting could release iron into the ocean to fuel algae blooms that would absorb carbon dioxide, has been debunked by new research. Scientists from Rutgers University-New Brunswick studied the Dotson Ice Shelf in the Amundsen Sea and found that meltwater from the glaciers contributes far less iron to surrounding waters than previously thought. Instead, the majority of the iron comes from deep ocean water and sediments. The team found that only about 10% of the iron in the area comes from the meltwater itself, with 62% sourced from deep ocean water and 28% from sediments on the continental shelf. These findings challenge long-standing assumptions about the role of melting glaciers in reducing atmospheric carbon and may affect climate change models going forward. The research highlights that subglacial processes could be more important in contributing iron to the Southern Ocean than previously believed.

item.title: A New Cellular Atlas Shows How Aging Affects the Body Across Multiple Organs
item.description: Scientists at Rockefeller University have created a groundbreaking cellular atlas that maps how aging affects cells in 21 organs of the body. This study, based on nearly 7 million cells from mice at different ages, reveals that aging is a synchronized process that begins earlier than previously thought. The research challenges the traditional view that aging is a random process, instead showing that specific cell types change in number over time. A significant finding is that aging-related changes in cells are coordinated across organs, and these shifts vary between males and females. Additionally, the study identified specific genetic 'hotspots'—regions of the genome that are particularly vulnerable to aging and could be targeted for anti-aging therapies. The researchers also discovered that immune signaling molecules like cytokines could potentially slow down the aging process across multiple organs. This research opens the door for developing interventions that could prevent age-rel

item.title: Scientists Find New Antibiotic Target in Bacteria, Offering Hope for Superbug Treatment
item.description: Researchers from Caltech have discovered a promising new target for antibiotics in the fight against drug-resistant bacteria, which are responsible for tens of thousands of deaths annually. The study reveals how bacteriophages, viruses that infect bacteria, target MurJ, a protein crucial for constructing the bacterial cell wall. These viruses use specialized proteins, known as single-gene lysis proteins (Sgls), to lock MurJ in a fixed position, preventing it from completing its function and causing bacterial death. The findings, published in *Nature*, show that multiple unrelated viruses evolved similar strategies to inhibit MurJ, highlighting it as a potential target for future antibiotics. This discovery is seen as an important step in developing new treatments as existing antibiotics are losing effectiveness. The research points to the potential of utilizing phages to discover new antibacterial treatments, marking a breakthrough in the ongoing battle against superbugs.

item.title: US and UK Resume Discussions on Tech Prosperity Deal, Prioritizing Civil Nuclear Collaboration
item.description:
The United States and United Kingdom have tentatively resumed negotiations on their 'tech prosperity deal,' originally announced in September 2025 during President Donald Trump's state visit to the UK. The agreement, which promotes collaboration in artificial intelligence, quantum computing, and nuclear energy, was paused in early December 2025 amid broader trade disputes. The Trump administration sought concessions on non-tariff barriers, such as regulations on food and industrial goods, leading to the suspension.
Recent talks, described as 'unsticking' the deal, have focused primarily on civil nuclear technologies. Senior officials from both nations discussed enhanced cooperation in this area and the possibility of co-hosting a summit on fusion technologies. This restart follows meetings at the World Economic Forum in Davos last month between the UK Prime Minister's special envoy Varun Chandra and his US counterpart Jamieson Greer, who agreed to prioritize nuclear aspects. Discussio

item.title: Trump's Attack on Iran Sparks Massive Regional Conflict with Global Repercussions
item.description: Donald Trump has made a bold and controversial decision to launch military action against Iran, aiming to overthrow the Iranian regime with the support of Israel. This move has escalated tensions in the Middle East and risks triggering a prolonged conflict that could last for weeks. Despite diplomatic talks in Geneva that were progressing, Trump chose to take extreme action, attacking Iran without seeking approval from the UN or Congress. The offensive is seen as a gamble, with potential consequences not only for the Middle East but for the global economy, as the region is a crucial oil trade route. Iran has promised a strong retaliation, viewing the attack as an existential threat. The conflict could deeply affect international relations, particularly for countries like Britain, where Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has expressed concerns over the legality of supporting the assault. Critics speculate that the attack might be driven more by Trump's domestic troubles, including economic

item.title: Iranian Prayer App Hack Sends Surrender Messages During Israeli-US Strikes
item.description: In the wake of joint Israeli and US airstrikes on Tehran, residents across Iran were shocked to receive mysterious push notifications from a hacked prayer-timing app, BadeSaba Calendar. These messages, which appeared shortly after explosions rocked the capital city, urged Iranian military personnel to surrender their weapons and join 'the forces of liberation' with promises of amnesty. The notifications, claiming that 'help is on the way,' targeted soldiers, calling them to abandon their posts or face retribution for their actions against innocent civilians. While the true source of the hack remains unknown, experts suggest this was a planned, nation-state-level cyber operation, likely carried out well in advance. The attack on the app, which boasts over 5 million downloads, represents a growing element of digital warfare in the conflict between Iran, Israel, and the US. Simultaneously, Iranians have been facing severe internet disruptions, making it difficult for civilians to communic

item.title: Figure Skating at the 2026 Winter Olympics: Key Moments and Future Impact
item.description: The 2026 Winter Olympics in Milano Cortina was filled with drama, surprises, and a new level of pressure in figure skating. With high stakes in every discipline, the event showed a clear shift in how skaters approach their performances. US skater Ilia Malinin, known as the 'Quad God,' entered the Games with high expectations, but his struggles with his signature quadruple axel left him off the podium. Meanwhile, US pair Madison Chock and Evan Bates, despite being favorites, ended up with a silver medal, overshadowed by the controversial French team, Guillaume Cizeron and Laurence Fournier Beaudry, who took gold in the ice dance competition. Retired figure skater Adam Rippon, who provided commentary throughout the event, discussed the evolving dynamics in the sport, the pressure skaters face, and how judging has shifted in recent years, making it harder for athletes to achieve perfect performances. He also shared his thoughts on the increasing political awareness of athletes, especially

item.title: Democratic Leaders' Role in Blocking War Powers Resolution on Trump’s Iran War Plans
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer and House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries are reportedly trying to prevent a War Powers Resolution aimed at blocking President Trump’s proposed military action against Iran. The resolution, which is a bipartisan effort, would force Congress to limit Trump’s authority to go to war with Iran. Despite their public silence, Schumer and Jeffries privately oppose the resolution, fearing the political fallout of opposing the war. Both leaders have been accused of prioritizing Israel’s interests, with their lack of vocal support for the resolution being seen as a way to avoid alienating pro-Israel lobby groups. The article criticizes Schumer and Jeffries for their perceived cynicism, arguing that they are willing to let Trump lead the country into a dangerous conflict while distancing themselves from the responsibility. The article also compares their stance to their support of similar resolutions in the past, such as one regarding Venezuela, where the poli

item.title: Xiaomi unveils new 17 Ultra smartphone, AirTag clone, and slim power bank at MWC 2026
item.description: Xiaomi has launched several new devices at the 2026 Mobile World Congress in Barcelona, including its flagship 17 Ultra smartphone, a new AirTag-like tracker, an ultra-slim power bank, and the Xiaomi Watch 5 smartwatch. The 17 Ultra, co-branded with Leica, features a 200-megapixel telephoto camera, a 50-megapixel ultrawide camera, and a Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor. The phone also supports Hypercharge technology for fast charging and wireless charging at 50W. Additionally, Xiaomi released a special Leica edition of the 17 Ultra to mark Leica’s 100th anniversary. The company also introduced an AirTag-like tracker, priced at €14.90, which works with both Apple’s Find My and Google’s Find Hub. Xiaomi's new ultra-slim power bank, just 6mm thick, charges at 22.5W wired and 15W wirelessly. The 5,000 mAh power bank comes in multiple color options and is priced between €59.99 and €64.99. Other products launched include the Xiaomi Watch 5, a new electric scooter, and various photography a

item.title: How Renewable Energy Is Shaping the Future of Power Generation
item.description: The article discusses how the growing integration of renewable energy is reshaping traditional energy systems. In particular, it highlights efforts by China and France to retrofit coal and nuclear plants to function intermittently, moving away from the traditional model of baseload power generation. This shift reflects how renewables, coupled with energy storage, are gradually displacing fossil fuel generation in regions like California. While natural gas is still the dominant choice for new power plants in the U.S., coal plants are being granted short-term life extensions. However, new nuclear power plants are seen as economically uncompetitive compared to renewable alternatives, posing challenges for the future of nuclear energy. The article also touches on the broader implications of these shifts for the energy market and climate change mitigation, as traditional power generation methods face increasing pressure from cleaner, more sustainable options.

item.title: Upcoming Webinar Critiques Canada's Small Modular Reactor Initiatives and Spending
item.description: A free webinar titled 'An Assessment of SMR Projects: The Case of Canada' is scheduled for Wednesday, March 18, 2026, at 1:00 PM EST (2:00 PM Atlantic Time). Hosted by Nuclear Transparency Watch based in Paris, France, and co-hosted by the Sustainability Learning Lab at St. Thomas University in Fredericton, New Brunswick, Canada, the event is open to everyone and requires registration via Zoom. The speakers are Susan O’Donnell, PhD, an Adjunct Research Professor and lead investigator of the CEDAR project at St. Thomas University, and M.V. Ramana, PhD, Professor and Simons Chair in Disarmament, Global and Human Security at the University of British Columbia. Madis Vasser, PhD, a senior SMR expert from Friends of the Earth Estonia, will moderate. The discussion will examine the progress of small modular reactors (SMRs) in Canada since the 2018 introduction of a federal-provincial strategic plan to develop them nationwide. Despite more than $4.5 billion invested by federal and provincial

item.title: NED President Caught Boasting About Role in Smuggling Starlink Terminals to Iran During Congressional Testimony
item.description: Damon Wilson, president of the National Endowment for Democracy (NED), made controversial remarks during a February 2026 House oversight hearing, boasting about his organization’s role in deploying Starlink terminals to Iran amid unrest. Wilson claimed that the NED had been instrumental in ensuring secure communication channels, including Starlink systems, which facilitated information flow both into and out of Iran during violent protests. His comments were interrupted by Rep. Lois Frankel, who urged him not to elaborate further. The NED’s involvement with the controversial technology comes amid claims that it had funded networks to smuggle the devices into the country. While some media outlets cited inflated casualty numbers from protests, Wilson's acknowledgment of the NED's direct involvement raised concerns about the transparency of such operations. Critics argue that the NED’s covert operations in Iran mirror the kind of destabilizing activities that the agency once claimed to op

item.title: U.S. Suspends Visa Processing for Citizens of 75 Countries Amid Stricter Screening
item.description: In a significant move, the U.S. State Department has suspended both immigrant and non-immigrant visa processing for citizens of 75 countries, effective January 21, 2026. The indefinite suspension applies to all types of visas, including tourist, student, work, and immigrant visas. Among the countries affected, notable inclusions are Egypt, Brazil, Russia, Iran, and Somalia—regions and powers not typically subject to blanket visa freezes. This decision follows a memo directing stricter screening based on factors like health, age, English proficiency, and financial resources, under the 'public charge' provision of U.S. immigration law. While framed as an administrative review, this freeze expands and consolidates existing security-based travel restrictions. Affected nations span Africa, Latin America, Eastern Europe, and the Middle East, with large populations and established diplomatic ties with the U.S. The pause has raised questions regarding its geopolitical implications, especially

item.title: Universities Face Backlash Over Faculty Connections to Epstein
item.description: The release of new Department of Justice (DOJ) documents detailing Jeffrey Epstein’s extensive connections within academia has caused a stir across U.S. universities. Emails spanning years show that Epstein’s influence went beyond financial contributions, implicating faculty members and university administrators in controversial ties to the disgraced financier. These revelations have sparked protests and calls for accountability from students, faculty, and alumni alike. While some professors, like David A. Ross at the School of Visual Arts in New York, have faced intense scrutiny over their correspondence with Epstein, others, like Mark Tramo at UCLA, are under fire for their ongoing professional relationships with him despite his known criminal history. Some institutions, such as UCLA and Harvard, are facing internal reviews and student activism, with some faculty resigning amid the fallout. However, other schools like Boston University and Bard College have downplayed or dismissed th

item.title: Scientists Develop Method to Convert Methane into Valuable Medicines and Chemicals
item.description: A team of scientists has achieved a significant breakthrough by finding a way to transform methane, a key component of natural gas, into valuable chemical building blocks for medicines and other products. The research, led by Martín Fañanás at the Centre for Research in Biological Chemistry and Molecular Materials (CiQUS) at the University of Santiago de Compostela, introduces an innovative process that uses light and an iron-based catalyst to activate methane molecules, making them reactive. This method could help reduce reliance on traditional fossil fuels by turning abundant methane into high-value compounds. In a landmark achievement, the team successfully synthesized dimestrol, a hormone therapy drug, directly from methane. The process, called allylation, adds a chemical fragment to methane, which can then be further processed into various products, including pharmaceuticals. The breakthrough also uses an environmentally friendly, low-cost iron catalyst and LED light, making the m

item.title: Trump's Major Military Action in Iran: Risks and Uncertain Outcome
item.description: President Donald Trump's decision to launch 'Operation Epic Fury' against Iran represents one of the most significant gambles made by an American president since the Iraq War. Initially elected on a platform opposing the 'forever wars', Trump has escalated tensions by aiming to cripple Iran’s military capabilities, particularly targeting its nuclear weapons program, missile systems, and navy. Despite previous strikes from the U.S. and Israel degrading Iran’s military strength, Tehran is retaliating fiercely, demonstrating that it is prepared to endure further conflict. Trump's approach relies heavily on air power and a deterrent of overwhelming military force, while avoiding large-scale ground involvement. However, he has warned that U.S. casualties could occur, a tradeoff he believes is necessary to neutralize the Iranian threat. With U.S. military forces in the region already on alert, questions remain about how the situation will evolve, particularly if the regime’s collapse leads t

item.title: Security Flaw in Robot Vacuums Exposes Privacy Risks for 6,700 Users
item.description: A security vulnerability in robot vacuums equipped with cameras and microphones has raised significant privacy concerns. Sammy Azdoufal, a robot vacuum owner, discovered that by experimenting with his own DJI Romo vacuum, he was able to hijack control of 6,700 similar vacuums across 24 countries. The flaw allowed him to access floor plans and audio-video feeds of users' homes using just the vacuum's serial number. This incident highlights the potential dangers of Internet-of-Things (IoT) devices that can move around private spaces. After the issue was publicly revealed, DJI quickly patched the vulnerability. While the company took action, experts warn that many other IoT gadgets, particularly those with cameras or microphones, could be similarly vulnerable. This breach underscores the need for better security protocols in devices that interact with our homes.

item.title: Elon Musk Criticizes OpenAI's Safety Record in Legal Testimony
item.description: In a recently revealed deposition, Elon Musk has sharply criticized OpenAI’s safety measures, highlighting concerns over the mental health consequences of its AI, ChatGPT. Musk, who founded the AI company xAI, claimed that no suicides have been linked to his company's Grok AI, unlike those attributed to ChatGPT. His comments were part of a larger argument during questioning about a 2023 open letter Musk signed urging AI labs to pause development of more powerful systems. Musk’s criticism also relates to a shift OpenAI made from a non-profit to a for-profit model, suggesting that commercial interests could compromise AI safety. Musk further recounted his concerns about Google’s dominance in AI, which was a major factor in the formation of OpenAI. However, Musk’s own xAI has faced controversies, including the distribution of non-consensual nude images generated by Grok, which led to investigations by authorities in the U.S. and Europe. The deposition marks a critical moment in Musk’s ong

item.title: Ancient chemical evidence points to sea sponges as Earth's first animals
item.description: A study from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) has uncovered chemical evidence suggesting that the first animals on Earth were likely ancient sea sponges. The team found molecular traces, called steranes, in rocks that are over 541 million years old. These compounds match those produced by modern demosponges, a major group of sea sponges. The research strengthens the idea that these early sponges existed long before other animal groups began to diversify during the Cambrian period. The study builds on previous findings from 2009, which showed similar compounds in Ediacaran rocks, and adds new evidence, including C31 steranes that match those produced by living sponges today. By studying both modern sponges and synthetic lab-made molecules, the researchers confirmed that these ancient chemical markers were biological, not geological in origin. This discovery highlights the significant role sea sponges may have played in the early evolution of animal life. The team plans to

item.title: New Book Highlights Radiation Risks and Safety for Professionals
item.description: A new book, 'The Dangers of Ionising Radiation', authored by Dr. Ian Fairlie, provides an in-depth, scientific guide aimed at government agencies, legal professionals, and medical clinicians. Released on February 25, 2026, the book delves into the risks associated with ionizing radiation, drawing attention to the critical need for proper understanding and management of these risks. With notable historical incidents like Chernobyl and Fukushima in mind, Dr. Fairlie examines the potential consequences of radiation exposure in various industries and environments. The book aims to equip professionals with the knowledge necessary to make informed decisions regarding radiation-related health and safety. Additionally, it serves as a vital resource for legal professionals dealing with radiation lawsuits and medical clinicians managing patients exposed to radiation. It is expected to become a key text for understanding radiation's scientific impact and its ethical and health implications.

item.title: India Blocks Access to Developer Platform Supabase Amid Unclear Restrictions
item.description: Supabase, a popular developer database platform, has been facing significant disruptions in India after a blocking order was issued on February 24, 2026. This move, directed by the Indian government under Section 69A of the Information Technology Act, led to inconsistent access to the platform across various networks in the country. Supabase, which serves as a key resource for developers and startups, acknowledged the issue and is working to resolve it through multiple channels. Despite the platform's main website remaining accessible, its core developer infrastructure has been largely inaccessible, causing disruptions for developers relying on it for their work. The platform, which has experienced rapid growth, particularly in India, has seen a sharp rise in traffic, with Indian visits growing by 179% in the past year. However, as of the latest updates, Supabase continues to face accessibility issues through major networks like JioFiber and Airtel. The reason for the blocking remains

item.title: O2 Becomes First UK Network to Offer Starlink Satellite Access for Rural Customers
item.description: O2 has become the first mobile network in the UK to enable satellite connectivity for its users, using Elon Musk's Starlink technology. This new service will automatically connect customers to Starlink satellites when traditional networks, such as 4G, are unavailable. The move is expected to enhance coverage in rural areas, potentially covering a region as large as two-thirds of Wales. However, the service is only available to a limited number of customers at launch, and compatibility is restricted to specific Samsung Galaxy devices. Additionally, the service comes at an extra cost, with an additional £3 per month for O2 customers. While the service has the potential to address coverage issues in areas lacking good mobile data, tech experts caution that it will take time for the benefits to be fully felt, and many customers may experience a delay before they can access the new feature. Some limited apps, such as Facebook Messenger and WhatsApp, will work with the satellite connection.

item.title: Ontario’s Nuclear Expansion: Funding the $400 Billion Plan
item.description: Ontario's nuclear expansion plans, proposed by Premier Doug Ford, include the construction of new nuclear power plants and refurbishments of existing reactors. The estimated capital cost exceeds $400 billion, which raises the critical question of how it will be financed. One option is to raise electricity rates significantly, leading to higher consumer costs. This could jeopardize the affordability of energy, Ontario’s economic competitiveness, and any decarbonization goals. Another strategy might involve hiding the capital costs as debt, reminiscent of the 1960s-1990s approach, which left Ontario Hydro financially unstable. Recently, a 29% electricity rate hike was linked to the financing of ongoing nuclear projects, with the government increasing its electricity rebate program to offset the impact on households. However, this subsidy has ballooned to $8.5 billion annually, diverting funds from other essential services. If Ontario's nuclear plans continue to expand, the financial burd

item.title: Microbe Defies Key Rule in Genetic Code, Offering New Insights into Protein Production
item.description: Researchers from the University of California, Berkeley, have discovered a microbe that challenges a key rule of the genetic code. Methanosarcina acetivorans, a methane-producing archaeon, interprets a normally 'stop' codon (UAG) in two ways. Sometimes, it halts protein production, while other times, it inserts a rare amino acid, pyrrolysine, allowing the protein to continue growing. This flexibility leads to two different proteins from the same genetic code, influenced by environmental factors. This phenomenon may be beneficial to the microbe, aiding in the breakdown of methylamine, a compound linked to human health and found in the gut. The discovery could also inform medical strategies for genetic disorders caused by premature stop codons, like cystic fibrosis and Duchenne muscular dystrophy, by enabling cells to bypass faulty stop signals. The study opens up new possibilities for genetic research and protein synthesis, suggesting that life might benefit from genetic ambiguity rathe

item.title: DHS Moves Toward Consolidating Biometric Data Across Agencies
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is planning to consolidate its biometric technologies into a unified platform that would allow multiple agencies to search through large databases of faces, fingerprints, iris scans, and other identifiers. This would include agencies such as Customs and Border Protection (CBP), Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), and the Transportation Security Administration (TSA), which currently use different systems. The new system would improve data sharing among agencies, enabling better coordination for watch-listing and enforcement actions. However, creating such a system may be challenging due to compatibility issues between existing technologies used across agencies. DHS is also exploring the use of voiceprints, though their use is legally contested and raises concerns about privacy. Critics argue that this expansion of biometric surveillance could infringe on civil liberties, particularly among vulnerable communities such as immigrants, politi

item.title: Airlines Suspend Flights in the Middle East Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Several major international airlines, including Air France, British Airways, Lufthansa, and KLM, have begun canceling flights to and from key Middle Eastern countries, including Israel, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE, and some parts of Egypt. The cancellations, which are expected to last through the weekend and potentially beyond, have been attributed to increasing regional tensions. Despite this, no official travel bans or escalations have been announced, and commercial aviation typically does not act on rumors. More airlines are likely to follow suit as the situation develops. The cancellations come at a time of heightened concern due to the political and military unrest in the region. However, these actions are not yet tied to any direct government mandates but reflect the volatile climate of the area. Travelers should stay informed about further updates as more disruptions could follow.

item.title: The Debate on Conscious AI: Can Machines Ever Achieve Consciousness?
item.description: In his new book, 'A World Appears,' Michael Pollan explores the growing conversation around conscious AI. Although AI systems have become incredibly advanced, Pollan argues that they can never truly achieve human-like consciousness. The debate over artificial consciousness gained momentum after the 2023 'Butlin Report' suggested that there are no obvious barriers to building conscious machines. However, Pollan highlights the challenges and moral dilemmas involved in this possibility, questioning whether machines could ever experience subjective awareness or feelings. He critiques the 'computational functionalism' theory that suggests consciousness could be replicated in machines, emphasizing the difference between biological brains and computer algorithms. As AI becomes increasingly capable in fields like chess and mathematics, Pollan reflects on how humanity might redefine itself if machines were to challenge our monopoly on consciousness. The philosophical implications of AI consciou

item.title: Britain's Nuclear Expansion: A Call for Public Protest Against Dangerous Military Investments
item.description: The article discusses Britain's controversial nuclear expansion, particularly the purchase of US nuclear-capable F-35A fighter jets, which are set to be stationed at RAF Marham in Norfolk. The UK's decision to modernize its nuclear-armed submarine fleet and acquire advanced nuclear-capable jets has sparked widespread opposition. Critics, including Green Party leaders and various civil society groups, argue that these actions undermine international disarmament efforts, breach the nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty, and increase the risk of nuclear conflict. The article also highlights the significant financial burden the project places on the British public, with costs expected to exceed £71 billion. Calls for public protest, including a demonstration at RAF Marham, aim to pressure the government to rethink its nuclear expansion policies and prioritize diplomacy and de-escalation over military escalation.

item.title: CDC Struggles with Leadership Gaps Amid Ongoing Changes
item.description: The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is currently experiencing significant leadership turmoil, following a law change in 2023 that requires the CDC director to be confirmed by the Senate. Since the departure of acting director Jim O'Neill in February 2026, National Institutes of Health (NIH) director Jay Bhattacharya has taken over as acting director. This is just the latest in a series of disruptions, as the CDC has seen its leadership rotate frequently in recent years. Under President Trump's administration, several leadership changes have followed controversies, including firings of top officials and mass layoffs that have drained the agency’s workforce. The lack of a permanent CDC director is concerning to public health experts, who warn that the agency’s ability to respond to health emergencies like bioterrorism or outbreaks could be compromised. Critics also point to the political motivations behind these changes, including a push from Republicans to exert mo

item.title: Pentagon Reveals Active Use of Directed-Energy Weapons in Military Operations
item.description: For years, directed-energy weapons were often dismissed as science fiction or conspiracy theories, but the Pentagon's recent confirmation changes that narrative. These advanced technologies, including lasers and high-power microwave systems, have been deployed in military operations for several years. The Navy's HELIOS laser system and the Army's DE M-SHORAD are among the notable operational directed-energy weapons already in use, with some systems deployed as early as the mid-2010s. The Pentagon’s quiet confirmation highlights the increasing use of these systems for countering threats like drones and small boats. Though these technologies are often described as 'non-lethal,' their actual effects, like frying electronics or causing migraines, point to a more complex reality. Despite years of development and deployment, the public was largely unaware of these weapons until recent disclosures buried in bureaucratic documents. The Pentagon's approach mirrors past government practices of d

item.title: Mexico's Navy Seizes Narco-Submarine with 4 Tons of Cocaine in Major Drug Bust
item.description: In a significant operation, the Mexican Navy has intercepted a semi-submersible vessel, also known as a 'narco-submarine,' carrying nearly four tons of cocaine. The seizure, which occurred near Manzanillo, Colima, adds to a series of recent drug busts in the Pacific Ocean, highlighting increased efforts to combat transnational drug trafficking. This operation was supported by the United States through intelligence sharing and joint efforts with agencies such as the Joint Interagency Task Force. The drugs, which were packed in 179 individual packages, were destined for the U.S. market, as part of a broader network using maritime routes to smuggle narcotics from Colombia and Ecuador. The Mexican Navy has significantly increased its surveillance and enforcement in these regions, with recent operations leading to the confiscation of over 10 tons of cocaine in just one week. Mexican officials emphasize that these actions not only disrupt the drug trade but also protect the safety of local c

item.title: Epstein Survivors Demand Accountability, Reject Claims of Trump's Exoneration
item.description: The ongoing investigations into the Epstein affair have sparked heated debates, particularly regarding the involvement of high-profile figures like Donald Trump. Republican Representatives Nancy Mace and Anna Paulina Luna have claimed that Epstein's victims have 'exonerated' Trump, but this assertion is not supported by survivors. Epstein survivor Dani Bensky refutes this claim, emphasizing that there are still leads and evidence that require further investigation. In fact, many survivors, like Jess Michaels, insist that Trump, a former associate of Epstein, remains a person of interest and should testify. This controversy risks undermining the survivors' pursuit of justice and feeds into a narrative that Republicans are offering Trump protection. Survivors continue to call for accountability, not just from Trump but from all individuals implicated in Epstein’s criminal activities, making it clear that exoneration claims do not reflect the truth of their experiences. As investigations

item.title: Guide to Watching the Total Lunar Eclipse on March 3, 2026
item.description: On March 3, 2026, a total lunar eclipse, often called a ‘blood moon,’ will take place. This rare event occurs when the Earth aligns between the Sun and the Moon, casting a reddish hue across the lunar surface. It will be visible in North and Central America, while only a partial view will be available in parts of Central and South Asia. The eclipse will start early in the morning, but the most striking phase, totality, will happen just before dawn. The reddish color of the moon is caused by sunlight passing through the Earth’s atmosphere, which filters and scatters the light. The event will last for just 12 minutes, and the moon will be low on the horizon, so finding a high vantage point free of obstructions is essential. The phenomenon is safe to view without special equipment, though it’s best enjoyed from a clear, open space. This occurrence is an exciting event for astronomy enthusiasts, as total lunar eclipses happen less frequently than solar eclipses, occurring roughly every 2.5

item.title: Trump Administration Blacklists Anthropic Over AI Military Use Dispute
item.description: The Trump administration has taken a bold step by blacklisting Anthropic, a leading AI lab, after the company refused to allow unfettered access to its AI technology for military purposes. The issue at hand centers around Anthropic’s stance on ‘red lines’ for AI use, particularly in military operations. Anthropic's CEO, Dario Amodei, has demanded that their AI technology not be used for mass surveillance or lethal automated attacks without human oversight. While Anthropic had previously supported the Pentagon's operations, including the use of its AI in the Pentagon’s 'Maven Smart System,' the company’s refusal to support certain military uses has led to this conflict. The administration, led by Trump and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, has responded by declaring Anthropic a ‘Supply-Chain Risk,’ a designation usually reserved for foreign companies. This has sparked outrage in the tech community, with AI leaders like Sam Altman of OpenAI expressing solidarity with Anthropic. The incident

item.title: The Changing Nature of Remembrance: From Honor to Loyalty Tests
item.description: In the article, the author argues that remembrance, particularly around events like the Holocaust, has shifted from being a solemn act of honoring victims to a performative loyalty test. The piece highlights how the current cultural and political climate demands specific types of remembrance, with an emphasis on certain narratives and prescribed framings. Missing a word or not highlighting the right aspect of remembrance is often viewed as an indictment of one's moral stance, even if the original intent was sincere. The author contends that this perpetual dissatisfaction is the foundation of moral leverage in today's society, where outrage must always be renewable. The piece criticizes the pressure to conform to an ever-changing set of standards and suggests that no act of remembrance will ever be enough to satisfy those imposing these standards.

item.title: Password Managers Expose Hidden Vulnerabilities in Latest Study
item.description: Password managers, long touted as a reliable solution for securely managing online credentials, are facing new vulnerabilities. A study by ETH Zurich and USI Lugano security researchers has revealed that even widely-used password managers like Bitwarden, Dashlane, and LastPass may not be as secure as previously thought. The study highlights potential flaws in their cryptographic systems, particularly in cloud-based services that sync data across devices. These flaws could allow malicious actors—whether insiders or hackers—to gain access to users' password vaults, or even alter them. Although password managers often market 'zero knowledge' systems, claiming they can't access user data, the researchers uncovered weaknesses in these systems that could undermine the security of users' sensitive information. The vulnerabilities were found to be associated with key escrow systems, which allow password recovery, and could be exploited if certain features are enabled. While the flaws were foun

item.title: Legendary Singer-Songwriter Neil Sedaka Dies at 86
item.description: American singer-songwriter Neil Sedaka, famous for timeless hits like 'Breaking Up Is Hard to Do' and 'Laughter in the Rain,' has passed away at the age of 86. His family released a heartfelt statement expressing profound grief over the sudden loss of their beloved husband, father, and grandfather, describing him as a true rock and roll legend and an inspiration to millions, while emphasizing his qualities as an incredible human being who will be deeply missed. No specific cause of death or additional details were provided in the announcement. Sedaka was a pivotal figure in the Brill Building era of songwriting in New York, where he collaborated closely with his childhood friend and lyricist Howard Greenfield to create iconic tracks such as 'Happy Birthday Sweet Sixteen,' 'Calendar Girl,' and 'Oh! Carol.' After a period of lesser visibility, he experienced a successful resurgence in the 1970s with popular songs including 'Laughter in the Rain' and 'Bad Blood.' One of his compositions,

item.title: How Google Handles Subpoena Requests: Insights from the Epstein Files
item.description: The recent release of documents by the US Department of Justice, linked to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, provides an unprecedented look into how tech companies like Google respond to government subpoenas. Among the millions of documents are several grand jury subpoenas directed to Google, alongside data produced by the company about specific users and the company’s formal responses to these subpoenas. Google’s response processes are designed to protect user privacy while fulfilling legal obligations, with the company often pushing back against overbroad requests. The documents also reveal that subpoenas, including those requesting subscriber information, often come with legal restrictions preventing Google from notifying the subject. Despite these restrictions, Google’s policies typically allow users to be notified unless legally prohibited. Some documents show that Google has handed over basic subscriber details like email addresses, phone numbers, and IP activity, as allowe

item.title: The Spread of Incel Terminology and Its Impact on Social Media Culture
item.description: The article explores how incel terminology, initially confined to online communities of 'involuntarily celibate' men, has moved into broader internet discourse. Terms like 'looksmaxxing,' 'mogging,' and 'Chad' are now frequently used on mainstream social media platforms, often divorced from their original context of misogyny and self-hatred. The article traces this shift from incel spaces to larger online environments, comparing it to how other subcultural terms, like 'woke,' have been co-opted over time. The language of incels—once a way to define an isolated, aggrieved group—has now entered general discourse, often used ironically or without understanding of its original meaning. The most notable example of this shift is the rise of Clavicular, a 20-year-old social media personality who embodies and propagates 'looksmaxxing' and other incel ideas. While he promotes toxic masculinity and controversial ideologies, his viral videos have made terms like 'frame mogging' and 'height moggin

item.title: Exploring the Role of AI in BDSM Relationships
item.description: As AI becomes increasingly integrated into various aspects of human life, one of its surprising uses is in BDSM relationships. Some people are turning to chatbots to explore power dynamics, role-play, and punishments within dominant-submissive (dom-sub) relationships. For example, Alesandra Madison, in a 15-year marriage with her husband, incorporated AI into their kink practices, using ChatGPT for punishment suggestions and task allocation. The growing popularity of AI for BDSM is fueled by its availability, customizability, and the perceived safety it offers, especially for beginners. AI bots like Joi AI, Character.AI, and Replika are popular choices for users seeking companionship and role-play experiences that mimic human interactions, but many in the BDSM community remain divided. Some criticize AI bots for being emotionally hollow and not providing real emotional connection, which is essential in BDSM dynamics. Others, however, value the tool as a way to explore desires without j

item.title: Zimbabwe's Reserve Bank to introduce new ZWG banknotes in April
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) has announced the introduction of new ZWG banknotes, starting with the ZiG10, ZiG20, and ZiG50 denominations, which will begin circulating on April 7, 2026. The ZiG100 and ZiG200 notes will follow at a later date, based on the central bank's assessment. The newly designed notes feature images of Africa's Big Five wildlife species, adding a cultural touch to the country's currency. These new notes will circulate alongside existing banknotes, while worn-out notes will be gradually phased out. A statutory instrument formalizing the introduction of the new currency will be published soon. Banks are required to return old notes to the RBZ for destruction, and in exchange, they will receive the new notes at equivalent values. The RBZ has also emphasized that no additional reserve money will be introduced; instead, the central bank will exchange electronic balances for physical currency. As part of the rollout, the RBZ will launch a nationwide education camp

item.title: NASA Postpones Artemis II Lunar Mission Launch to April Due to Rocket Issue
item.description: NASA has delayed the launch of Artemis II, the crewed lunar flyby mission, yet again, due to a technical issue with the Space Launch System (SLS) rocket. Originally scheduled for March 6, the new target date is now set for April. The problem stems from a failure in the helium flow of the rocket’s interim cryogenic propulsion stage, which is vital for purging engines and pressurizing fuel tanks. Despite two successful dress rehearsals earlier in the month, the issue arose during routine operations. As a result, engineers will need to make repairs in the Vehicle Assembly Building, making the March launch window unfeasible. While NASA remains optimistic about the April window, much depends on repair timelines and data from ongoing efforts. Artemis II had already been delayed once earlier in February due to hydrogen leaks detected during a wet dress rehearsal. The mission is crucial for NASA’s return to lunar exploration, even though no landing is planned during Artemis II, as that will be

item.title: Paramount Skydance Agrees to Acquire Warner Bros Discovery in $110 Billion Deal After Netflix Withdraws Bid
item.description: Paramount Skydance has finalized a $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros Discovery, ending a competitive bidding war that involved Netflix. Netflix had initially agreed to acquire certain assets but declined to match Paramount's superior offer of $31 per share in cash, leading to its withdrawal. The deal, announced on February 27, 2026, unites two major Hollywood legacy studios and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, pending regulatory approvals and shareholder votes. Paramount, now led by David Ellison (son of billionaire Larry Ellison), will gain control of Warner Bros Discovery's extensive assets, including the Warner Bros film studio, HBO Max streaming service (to merge with Paramount+), iconic franchises like Harry Potter, Superman, Barbie, DC Comics, and Game of Thrones, as well as news networks CNN and other linear TV properties. This complements Paramount's existing portfolio, featuring titles such as Top Gun, The Godfather, and the Paramount+ platform. David E

item.title: Pentagon Takes Action Against Anthropic Over AI Concerns
item.description: The Pentagon has decided to designate Anthropic, the AI company, as a 'Supply-Chain Risk' following its refusal to allow its technology to be used for mass surveillance or autonomous weapon systems. President Trump directed all federal agencies to cease using Anthropic products, effective within six months. This decision came after a public dispute between the company and the Department of Defense, which criticized Anthropic for placing limitations on its AI models. Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei defended the company's stance, emphasizing that they preferred to continue supporting the Department of Defense with certain safeguards in place. Despite this, the Department of Defense, led by Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth, followed through on the president's directive, officially making it clear that no contractor or partner working with the U.S. military could continue business with Anthropic. This move highlights the increasing tensions surrounding the ethical use of AI technology, particu

item.title: Israeli National Arrested for Operating Illegal Bio-Lab in Las Vegas Airbnb Garage
item.description: In a shocking discovery, an illegal biological laboratory was found inside an Airbnb garage in Las Vegas. The lab, run by Israeli citizen Ori Solomon, was raided by the FBI, leading to the seizure of over 1,000 biological samples of unknown substances. These included fridges filled with vials of unidentifiable liquids, centrifuges, and gallon jugs of red and brown materials. First responders and cleaners fell seriously ill after being exposed to the lab's contents, though there has been no public health advisory or declaration of a bioweapon risk. While no immediate threat has been declared, the FBI continues to investigate the samples in their labs. The lab operator, Ori Solomon, was found to possess multiple IDs under different names, as well as Israeli and French passports. Investigators have also linked this incident to a similar bio-lab found in Reedley, CA, which was involved with dangerous biological agents. Despite the potential risks posed by such labs, questions remain about

item.title: OpenAI terminates employee for alleged insider trading on prediction markets
item.description: OpenAI recently fired an employee for using confidential company information in connection with trades made on prediction markets, including Polymarket. These markets allow individuals to wager on outcomes of real-world events, such as product launches and IPO timelines, with significant sums of money potentially involved. The company did not disclose the identity of the employee but emphasized that this breach violated OpenAI's strict policy against using insider information for personal gain. This incident highlights the growing concerns around insider trading in financial platforms like prediction markets, which are sometimes viewed as alternatives to traditional betting sites. Prediction markets, such as Polymarket and Kalshi, are gaining popularity, but they have raised regulatory questions, especially after recent cases of alleged insider trading, including an incident involving a MrBeast editor. OpenAI’s swift action against the employee is part of an ongoing trend of companies

item.title: Study Links Higher Cancer Mortality Rates to Proximity of Nuclear Power Plants in the U.S.
item.description: A study published in February 2026 reveals that counties in the U.S. located closer to operational nuclear power plants have higher cancer mortality rates compared to those farther away. The study, which analyzed nationwide mortality data from 2000 to 2018, highlights significant cancer risks in populations living near nuclear plants, particularly among older adults. While the research does not confirm direct causality, it points to the need for further investigation into how exposure to radiation from nuclear plants may contribute to long-term health problems. Researchers observed stronger correlations in cancer mortality among men aged 65-74 and women aged 55-64. Nuclear power plants emit radioactive pollutants that can disperse through air, water, and soil, potentially exposing nearby populations to chronic low-level ionizing radiation. This study adds to a growing body of evidence calling for more comprehensive health assessments around nuclear facilities. The research also undersc

item.title: OPPO's New Shenzhen Headquarters: A Glimpse into the Future of Tech Workspaces
item.description: Designed by Zaha Hadid Architects, the new OPPO headquarters in Shenzhen is a bold statement of futuristic design. The building, which began development in 2020, consists of four interconnected high-rises, each nearly 200 meters tall, and is nearing completion. The structure is a vertical campus, rather than a traditional office tower, and features sky bridges linking the buildings together, along with a 20-story atrium that creates an expansive, canyon-like feeling. The design prioritizes flexibility with column-free floor plans, panoramic views, and open spaces that encourage movement and spontaneous interaction between employees. More than just an office, the headquarters blends public areas, workplace, and a landmark structure into one seamless design. It feels like an embodiment of how future tech companies might combine work and public space, focusing on the idea of fluidity and openness. OPPO's headquarters in Shenzhen is set to redefine modern office environments by breaking tr

item.title: New DOJ Revelations Raise Questions About Epstein's Death and the Surveillance Footage
item.description: The death of Jeffrey Epstein remains one of the most debated and mysterious events of recent years, especially due to the strange circumstances surrounding the night of August 9, 2019. Three primary theories have emerged: Epstein's alleged suicide, a cover-up orchestrated by powerful figures, or a more bizarre explanation involving a body swap or decoy. The DOJ recently released new documents shedding light on the surveillance footage from the night Epstein died, which revealed an 'orange-colored shape' moving up a staircase toward the isolated tier where Epstein's cell was located. The shape was initially described as possibly being an inmate escorted to that area, but the reports surrounding the footage are conflicting. Further investigation into Epstein's death reveals a string of irregularities including a missing noose, falsified records, and unmonitored periods when Epstein was left alone. With incomplete or manipulated footage, a lack of proper monitoring, and contradictory repo

item.title: Former Prince Andrew Arrested in UK on Misconduct Allegations
item.description: In a shocking development, former Prince Andrew Windsor has been arrested in the U.K. on suspicion of misconduct in public office. The 66-year-old royal, who has faced significant public scrutiny over his association with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, was taken into custody following allegations linked to his role in Epstein's crimes. Authorities are investigating claims that a second woman was allegedly sent by Epstein for a sexual encounter with Andrew, though he has consistently denied any wrongdoing. Despite the controversial nature of his previous associations, including allegations from Virginia Giuffre, who accused Andrew of sexual trafficking, the arrest marks an unprecedented move for the royal family. While no formal charges have been laid, the investigation into Andrew's conduct could signal a shift in how the British monarchy is held accountable for actions in the public office. In a statement, Assistant Chief Constable Oliver Wright emphasized the importance of s

item.title: Brady Tkachuk Denies Involvement in Fake TikTok Video Shared by White House
item.description: Brady Tkachuk, the captain of the NHL's Ottawa Senators and a member of Team USA, has publicly refuted claims that he made derogatory comments about Canadians in a TikTok video shared by the White House. The video, which includes fabricated audio and AI-generated media, falsely attributes offensive remarks to Tkachuk, with the audio suggesting he mocked Canadians by calling them 'maple syrup eating f***s'. Tkachuk, who was celebrating Team USA's gold medal win at the Winter Games in Italy, strongly denied that the voice in the video was his and clarified that such words would never come from him. He also denied being the voice heard shouting 'close the northern border' during a celebratory phone call with former President Donald Trump. The video, which includes a note stating it contains 'AI-generated media', was dismissed by Tkachuk as fake. He stressed that the video misrepresented him and that he is not in control of the White House TikTok account, where the video was shared. Tkachu

item.title: What the Ellison Family Will Control If Paramount Acquires Warner Bros.
item.description: Larry and David Ellison's media empire is set to expand further with a potential $110 billion deal between Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). If federal regulators approve the merger, the Ellisons will gain control of a massive array of intellectual properties, including iconic franchises like DC Comics, Harry Potter, and Star Trek. The deal, which would follow Skydance's $8 billion merger with Paramount less than a year ago, would place the Ellisons among the most influential media moguls in the world. Alongside owning CBS, HBO, CNN, and networks like Comedy Central and Showtime, they would also control some of Hollywood’s biggest blockbuster franchises. The potential acquisition includes distribution rights for The Lord of the Rings and Mission Impossible, as well as streaming rights for Avatar Studios' content. Additionally, Oracle, where Larry Ellison is the cofounder, recently acquired a stake in TikTok, adding even more cultural clout. The article details the sc

item.title: Empresarios emiratíes critican la escalada bélica impulsada por EE.UU. ante el impacto económico del conflicto con Irán
item.description: Los empresarios de los Emiratos Árabes Unidos expresan un creciente malestar hacia la política de confrontación de Donald Trump con Irán, que ha derivado en una guerra regional con graves consecuencias económicas. Desde hace casi un año, tras la visita de Trump a Abu Dabi con promesas de inversiones por 1,4 billones de dólares en sectores como inteligencia artificial, semiconductores y energía, la relación se ha deteriorado por los ataques con misiles y drones iraníes contra infraestructuras del Golfo. Estos han sido mayoritariamente interceptados, pero han dañado la percepción de estabilidad y seguridad que tanto ha costado construir en la región. El multimillonario dubaití Khalaf Al Habtoor ha criticado abiertamente en X al presidente estadounidense, preguntando quién le dio autoridad para arrastrar al Golfo a un conflicto no deseado. El Estrecho de Ormuz está prácticamente paralizado, lo que ha disparado los precios del petróleo hacia su mayor subida semanal desde 2022 y ha afectado

item.title: La UCO informa al juez Peinado que no hay coincidencias en vuelos internacionales de Begoña Gómez y su asesora Cristina Álvarez
item.description:
El juez Juan Carlos Peinado, que investiga desde abril de 2024 a Begoña Gómez, esposa del presidente del Gobierno, por presuntos delitos como tráfico de influencias, corrupción en los negocios, malversación y apropiación indebida, ha recibido un nuevo revés. La Unidad Central Operativa (UCO) de la Guardia Civil ha analizado los vuelos internacionales de Begoña Gómez y su asesora en Moncloa, Cristina Álvarez, en los últimos cinco años, a petición del magistrado, quien buscaba posibles coincidencias en desplazamientos a países como República Dominicana, Congo, Guinea Ecuatorial o Rusia para determinar si Álvarez prestó asesoramiento en actividades privadas de Gómez.
El informe de la UCO concluye que no se han encontrado vuelos en los que ambas coincidieran. Solo se registra un viaje de Begoña Gómez a República Dominicana entre el 1 y el 3 de junio de 2022, sin la presencia de Álvarez. Esta última realizó viajes a México, Egipto y Estados Unidos en fechas distintas. Además, el artículo m

item.title: Nueve mujeres presentan denuncia contra un influencer por violencia machista digital
item.description: La asociación Stop Violencia Vicaria, junto a nueve mujeres, ha interpuesto una denuncia colectiva contra Hugo López Arteaga, un influencer acusado de difundir mensajes que incitan a la violencia contra mujeres y colectivos feministas. Según la Fiscalía Provincial de Guipúzcoa, los hechos podrían encuadrarse en delitos de odio, trato degradante y revelación de secretos. Entre los contenidos denunciados se incluyen vídeos y publicaciones donde el investigado trivializa la violencia machista, expone datos personales de usuarias y difunde acusaciones falsas, provocando hostigamiento y amenazas contra ellas. Tras el cierre de sus cuentas de Instagram, el influencer trasladó su actividad a TikTok, donde mantiene decenas de miles de seguidores. Las denunciantes han sufrido consecuencias tanto digitales como en su vida personal, incluyendo ansiedad, miedo y reducción de su actividad en redes. Expertas en violencia machista digital señalan que este tipo de agresiones dañan la reputación, invad

item.title: Nada es Gratis anuncia el cierre definitivo de su blog tras 17 años de actividad
item.description: El blog Nada es Gratis, un referente en análisis económico riguroso y debate informado en español, ha anunciado su cese definitivo de actividad. La decisión fue tomada por unanimidad por los socios de la Asociación Nada es Gratis en la Asamblea Extraordinaria celebrada el 12 de febrero de 2026, y entrará en vigor el 27 de marzo de 2026, coincidiendo con las vacaciones de Semana Santa. Tras una profunda reflexión, los editores y la junta directiva han considerado diversas alternativas, como reducir la frecuencia de publicaciones o explorar vías de financiación, pero han rechazado buscar patrocinadores para preservar su independencia. El esfuerzo sostenido durante años se ha basado en la vocación de contribuir al debate público con rigor académico, sin ánimo de lucro ni sesgos. Sin embargo, reconocen que el formato de blog independiente ha perdido relevancia frente al auge de las redes sociales, que ofrecen mayor alcance aunque con menos filtros y mayor tendencia al conflicto y la polari

item.title: Pastores evangélicos oran con Trump en el Despacho Oval durante la guerra contra Irán
item.description: En medio del séptimo día del conflicto armado entre Estados Unidos e Israel contra Irán, que comenzó el 28 de febrero de 2026 con una ofensiva masiva que ha causado miles de víctimas y daños extensos en infraestructuras civiles iraníes (más de 4.000 edificios afectados según la Media Luna Roja Iraní, incluyendo 3.646 hogares y 528 unidades comerciales, además de tres hospitales fuera de servicio), el presidente Donald Trump participó en una ceremonia íntima de oración en el Despacho Oval. Pastores evangélicos prominentes, como Robert Jeffress, Samuel Rodriguez, David Barton y Tom Mullins, se reunieron alrededor del escritorio Resolute, imponiendo manos sobre Trump mientras cerraban los ojos en oración. El pastor Mullins lideró una invocación emocional pidiendo gracia divina, protección para el presidente, para las tropas estadounidenses y para que Trump tenga la fuerza necesaria para guiar al país en tiempos de guerra, enfatizando la idea de 'una nación bajo Dios'. El vídeo y las imáge

item.title: Vicente Vallés critica la imagen de Trump rezando con evangélicos en la Casa Blanca y el 'No a la guerra' de Sánchez en medio de la crisis con EE.UU. por Irán
item.description: El periodista Vicente Vallés, en el programa Espejo Público de Antena 3, analizó la tensa situación diplomática entre España y Estados Unidos desatada por la postura del presidente Pedro Sánchez frente al conflicto con Irán. Vallés destacó el amplio consenso en España contra la participación en guerras, aunque reconoció el debate sobre el uso de bases españolas por parte de EE.UU. para ataques contra Irán. Criticó la mezcla de política interior y exterior en el lema 'No a la guerra' de Sánchez, que según él ha provocado riesgos innecesarios y ha complicado el consenso interno, aludiendo irónicamente a que los españoles no eligen al presidente estadounidense. Sobre Donald Trump, Vallés calificó de 'bochorno' la escena en la Casa Blanca donde el mandatario rezó rodeado de pastores evangélicos para reconciliarse con su base MAGA y evangélica, decepcionada porque Trump inició una contienda bélica incumpliendo promesas de no involucrarse en guerras. El analista ironizó diciendo que a Trump

item.title: Multimillonario emiratí Khalaf Al Habtoor critica a Trump por involucrar al Golfo en el conflicto con Irán
item.description: El empresario emiratí Khalaf Ahmad Al Habtoor, presidente del Grupo Al Habtoor y destacado multimillonario de Dubái, ha publicado una dura carta abierta dirigida al presidente de Estados Unidos, Donald Trump, en la que le cuestiona directamente su decisión de involucrar a la región del Golfo en un conflicto armado con Irán. El mensaje, difundido el 5 de marzo de 2026 en redes sociales, surge tras los ataques iraníes contra bases estadounidenses en Emiratos Árabes Unidos, en represalia por los bombardeos conjuntos de Estados Unidos e Israel sobre Teherán. Al Habtoor pregunta: «¿Quién te dio autoridad para arrastrar nuestra región a la guerra con Irán? ¿Y en base a qué tomaste esta peligrosa decisión?». Critica que no se haya calculado el daño colateral y subraya que los primeros en sufrir las consecuencias serán los países de la zona, colocados en el centro de un peligro no elegido. Recuerda las iniciativas de paz anunciadas por Trump que, según él, han quedado en nada ante esta escalad

item.title: Países del Golfo evalúan reducir inversiones y contratos con EE.UU. ante el impacto económico de la guerra con Irán
item.description:
Según un informe del Financial Times citado por Asian Mail, Arabia Saudí, Emiratos Árabes Unidos, Kuwait y Catar están debatiendo la posibilidad de reducir o retirar ciertos contratos e inversiones futuras con Estados Unidos. Esta medida se considera como precaución ante las tensiones económicas derivadas del conflicto en curso entre EE.UU./Israel e Irán, que ha generado inestabilidad regional y presiones sobre las economías del Golfo.
Un alto cargo del Golfo, que no especificó los países exactos pero indicó que tres de las cuatro grandes economías (Arabia Saudí, EAU, Kuwait y Catar) han mantenido discusiones conjuntas, explicó que se están revisando contratos existentes para evaluar si se pueden invocar cláusulas de fuerza mayor. El objetivo es aliviar la tensión presupuestaria causada por varios factores: la reducción de ingresos energéticos debido a la ralentización de la producción o problemas en el envío de crudo, el impacto negativo en sectores como el turismo y la aviación, y e

item.title: Rusia facilita a Irán datos de inteligencia sobre posiciones de fuerzas militares estadounidenses en Oriente Medio
item.description:
Según una exclusiva publicada por The Washington Post el 6 de marzo de 2026, Rusia está suministrando a Irán información de inteligencia destinada a facilitar ataques contra fuerzas estadounidenses en Oriente Medio. Esta asistencia incluye datos precisos sobre las ubicaciones de buques de guerra y aeronaves militares de Estados Unidos en la región. Tres funcionarios estadounidenses familiarizados con la inteligencia, que hablaron bajo condición de anonimato debido a la sensibilidad del asunto, han confirmado esta cooperación, que representa la primera evidencia clara de que un adversario importante de Washington, como Rusia, participa —aunque sea de forma indirecta— en el conflicto actual en la zona.
Esta revelación se produce en un contexto de escalada bélica entre Estados Unidos, Israel e Irán, donde se han producido ataques con drones y misiles por ambas partes. Por ejemplo, un dron iraní dañó recientemente un edificio próximo a la sede de la Quinta Flota de la Armada estadounidens

item.title: El X-85 Goblin: un caza experimental diseñado para operar desde bombarderos
item.description: El X-85 Goblin fue un proyecto experimental estadounidense desarrollado durante la Segunda Guerra Mundial como parte de un intento de crear un caza parásito capaz de ser transportado dentro del compartimento de un bombardero. Este diseño buscaba dotar a los bombarderos de defensa aérea adicional sin depender de aeródromos para su despegue y aterrizaje. Sin embargo, el proyecto enfrentó limitaciones técnicas importantes, especialmente por la inexistencia de un tren de aterrizaje convencional, lo que impedía al X-85 Goblin operar de manera autónoma en el campo de batalla. A pesar de su ingenio, la iniciativa nunca llegó a desplegarse en combate. La idea de un caza que pudiera ser lanzado y recuperado por un bombardero refleja la creatividad y la experimentación tecnológica de la época, aunque también evidencia los desafíos que suponían estas innovaciones. Este proyecto forma parte de la historia de la aviación militar y de los esfuerzos por integrar sistemas de defensa aérea innovadores,

item.title: Un actor desmiente el bulo viral sobre una supuesta excursión escolar islámica en Sevilla: era una ruta teatralizada histórica
item.description: Un vídeo grabado en las calles de Sevilla se viralizó rápidamente en redes sociales, especialmente tras ser compartido por la cuenta de Vox, donde se presentaba como una excursión escolar obligatoria para 'educar en el Ramadán'. En las imágenes se veía a un grupo de menores acompañados por un hombre vestido con indumentaria islámica, lo que generó indignación y comentarios como 'voy a vomitar' o acusaciones de ingeniería social e imposición islámica en horario lectivo. Sin embargo, el propio protagonista del vídeo, un actor profesional, aclaró la realidad: se trataba de una ruta teatralizada histórica organizada por la empresa Entre leyendas, que lleva más de diez años realizando estas actividades educativas con escolares. El personaje representado era el rey Al Mutámid, gobernante de Sevilla entre 1069 y 1091, en una recreación sobre la historia de la ciudad durante el periodo taifa. No había ninguna relación con el Ramadán ni con adoctrinamiento religioso islámico. El actor criticó d

item.title: Resumen de capítulos y temporadas de Ultrainternational Geographic
item.description: El contenido disponible de 'Ultrainternational Geographic' consiste principalmente en una lista de capítulos de sus distintas temporadas, desde la temporada 1 hasta la 21. Actualmente no hay capítulos disponibles de la temporada más reciente. La web organiza los contenidos por secciones como documentales, series y reportajes, y permite consultar ediciones recientes y colecciones de los últimos 10 días. Aunque se menciona la duración de un vídeo de 2 minutos, la información principal se centra en la estructura del catálogo y la disponibilidad de episodios, sin entrar en detalles sobre los contenidos específicos de los capítulos. En resumen, la noticia informa sobre la organización y el acceso al catálogo de la serie/documental, destacando que no hay material disponible de la temporada actual y ofreciendo un recorrido por temporadas anteriores, clips y otros contenidos audiovisuales del canal.

item.title: Kristi Noem cuestionada en el Congreso por contrato de 143 millones a empresa sin experiencia ni sede clara
item.description: La secretaria de Seguridad Nacional, Kristi Noem, compareció ante el Comité de Supervisión de la Cámara de Representantes y no supo responder preguntas clave sobre un contrato de 143 millones de dólares adjudicado sin licitación a Safe America Media, una empresa creada solo ocho días antes de recibir el encargo entre febrero y agosto de 2025. Esta compañía, sin sitio web, sin experiencia previa en contratos federales y vinculada a la dirección de un operador republicano veterano (Michael McElwain), produjo anuncios antiinmigrantes para ICE. El congresista Joe Neguse destacó la opacidad: el dinero fluyó a través de subcontratistas no públicos hacia firmas conectadas personalmente con Noem, como The Strategy Group, cuyo CEO está casado con la ex portavoz de Noem en DHS y que ha empleado a allegados suyos como Corey Lewandowski. Noem evitó dar detalles sobre la sede de la empresa y defendió el contrato, pero la falta de transparencia levantó sospechas de posible uso indebido de fondos púb

item.title: EE.UU. hunde con torpedo la fragata iraní IRIS Dena en el océano Índico tras ejercicio conjunto con India
item.description: El 4 de marzo de 2026, un submarino de la Armada estadounidense hundió la fragata iraní IRIS Dena en aguas internacionales del océano Índico, cerca de la costa de Sri Lanka, mediante un torpedo Mark 48. Este incidente marca el primer hundimiento de un buque enemigo por torpedo desde la Segunda Guerra Mundial. La nave, que formaba parte de la Marina iraní, regresaba del ejercicio naval multilateral MILAN 2026, organizado por la Armada india entre el 15 y el 26 de febrero, en el que participaron tanto Estados Unidos (con una aeronave de patrulla marítima) como Irán. Según normas del ejercicio, los buques no portaban munición, por lo que la Dena probablemente iba desarmada, algo que EE.UU. conocía al haber estado presente. El ataque causó al menos 87 muertos, 32 rescatados por la Marina de Sri Lanka y 61 desaparecidos; EE.UU. no respondió a la llamada de socorro. El secretario de Defensa Pete Hegseth celebró el hecho como una 'muerte silenciosa' y lo enmarcó en una política de ignorar 're

item.title: Usuarios anónimos de X denuncian búsquedas masivas de sus nombres reales en Israel tras verificar su identidad con Au10tix
item.description: Varios usuarios anónimos de la red social X (antes Twitter) han denunciado que, poco después de criticar las acciones de Israel en Palestina y verificar su identidad para acceder a funciones premium, sus nombres reales completos —que no habían divulgado en línea— aparecen con picos de búsquedas en Google desde Israel, según datos de Google Trends. Casos destacados incluyen usuarios como TransFemPOTUS, que reportó 11 búsquedas en un día, TheAtlantean9 y Bionico Bandito, quien afirmó 100 búsquedas tras publicar un contenido crítico. Muchos señalan como responsable a Au10tix, empresa israelí encargada de la verificación de identidad en X, que requiere subir fotos de documentos oficiales y selfies. Au10tix fue fundada por veteranos de la Unidad 8200 del ejército israelí (una unidad de inteligencia élite vinculada a ciberespionaje y operaciones como Pegasus o el ataque con buscapersonas en Líbano en 2024) y cuenta con numerosos exagentes en su plantilla. Aunque la compañía asegura borrar lo

item.title: El Gorafi publica una sátira sobre un supuesto misil estadounidense para 'imponer democracia' con mínimo impacto humano
item.description:
El medio satírico francés Le Gorafi publica una noticia humorística en la que afirma que un laboratorio del ejército estadounidense ha creado un misil revolucionario denominado 'Democratizator'. Según el artículo, este ingenio permitiría imponer la democracia en un país con menos de 750 muertos, una cifra muy inferior a las habituales en este tipo de intervenciones (que, según el texto irónico, suelen requerir al menos 10.000 fallecidos si la población desea la democracia, o hasta 150.000 si no la quiere).
El físico ficticio Ulysses Baratee explica que el secreto reside en eliminar de golpe a todas las élites (ejecutivas, culturales, políticas y económicas) del régimen antidemocrático, infligiéndoles además atroces sufrimientos para servir de ejemplo y disuasión. El tono es claramente paródico y exagerado, criticando de forma sarcástica las intervenciones militares estadounidenses justificadas bajo el pretexto de promover la democracia, a menudo asociadas a elevadas cifras de víctimas

item.title: Rutte reconoce que la OTAN refuerza la proyección de poder de Estados Unidos y la dependencia europea
item.description: El secretario general de la OTAN, Mark Rutte, ha reafirmado el papel central de Estados Unidos en la alianza atlántica al declarar que la OTAN actúa como plataforma para que Washington proyecte su poder global. Sus declaraciones se produjeron en un contexto de tensiones internacionales, especialmente en Oriente Próximo, y subrayan la asimetría histórica entre el liderazgo militar y político estadounidense y la dependencia estratégica de los países europeos. Rutte argumentó que, sin el apoyo de los aliados europeos, Estados Unidos habría tenido mayores dificultades para llevar a cabo su campaña contra la capacidad nuclear y de misiles de Irán. Esta postura genera debate sobre la autonomía estratégica de Europa y la capacidad de los países europeos para definir sus propias prioridades de defensa. La relación cercana de Rutte con Donald Trump, incluida la polémica en la que se refirió al presidente estadounidense como “daddy”, refuerza la percepción de una OTAN alineada con los intereses

item.title: Sellos postales de Bután que se reproducen como vinilos: historia y colección
item.description: En 1972, Bután lanzó una serie de sellos postales únicos que pueden reproducirse como pequeños discos de vinilo.
